User Story: As a new self-hosted operator, I want to create the initial site and owner account so I can enter the admin app.
Expected Behaviour: /admin probes setup status, renders setup when no owner/site exists, accepts site/account input, creates owner, starts a session, and redirects into the authenticated admin workspace.
Edge Cases: Existing setup redirects to login/admin; duplicate owner setup is rejected; weak/missing password or email blocks creation; DB errors surface as error envelopes.
Validation Rules: Request body is TypeBox-validated; password and email are server-validated; setup route only mutates before setup is complete; CSRF origin gate applies to state-changing admin APIs.
Known Assumptions: Disposable local DB can be reset during E2E; first accessible workspace depends on owner capabilities.
Test Cases: Happy: clean DB setup reaches admin. Error: duplicate setup rejected after owner exists. Boundary: minimal valid account/site fields. Invalid: empty email/password/site name. Permission: unauthenticated only before setup. Performance: setup boot shows skeleton then form. Mobile: setup form fits narrow viewport.
Current Status: Manual core lifecycle passed 2026-06-22; automated/build/lint/bundle/Playwright E2E passed; broader exploratory pending
Notes: Existing automated setup coverage is listed in docs/e2e/README.md. Manual run 2026-06-22 core-owner-lifecycle passed; see docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-22-core-owner-lifecycle.md.

User Story: As an admin user, I want to sign in with email and password so I can access my permitted workspaces.
Expected Behaviour: POST /admin/api/cms/login normalizes email, verifies password, records activity, creates session cookie, returns current user or pending MFA.
Edge Cases: Unknown email follows constant-time verification path; suspended/locked accounts cannot login; wrong password increments lockout state; route is 405 for wrong methods.
Validation Rules: Login body requires email and password strings; session cookie Path is /admin; auth activity result is recorded; CSRF origin gate applies.
Known Assumptions: Seeded owner credentials are local-only for tests.
Test Cases: Happy: valid login redirects to admin. Error: wrong password returns clear failure. Boundary: case-insensitive email. Invalid: missing password. Permission: disabled user blocked. Performance: no long blank state after submit. Mobile: login panel remains usable.
Current Status: Manual core lifecycle passed 2026-06-22; automated/build/lint/bundle/Playwright E2E passed; broader exploratory pending
Notes: Manual run 2026-06-22 core-owner-lifecycle passed; see docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-22-core-owner-lifecycle.md.

User Story: As an MFA-enabled admin, I want to complete second-factor verification after password login so the session becomes fully authenticated.
Expected Behaviour: Login returns mfaRequired and pending token when TOTP is enabled; POST /auth/mfa/verify validates code or recovery code, mints session, and records MFA pass time.
Edge Cases: Expired or invalid pending token fails; wrong code records mfa_failed; recovery codes are single-use; pending flow should not expose full admin.
Validation Rules: Verification body requires code string; TOTP secret and recovery codes are server-owned; cookies only issued after successful verification.
Known Assumptions: Browser E2E enables MFA from the displayed setup key, then verifies empty-code required-field validation, wrong-code feedback, generated TOTP login, mobile 390px challenge containment, pending-cookie API denial, expired pending-session rejection, unknown pending-cookie rejection, recovery-code login burn, and rejection of the same recovery code on reuse in disposable owner sessions.
Test Cases: Happy: valid TOTP completes login. Error: empty submit keeps the MFA challenge visible, focuses the required field, and exposes a native validation message; wrong login code shows inline invalid-code feedback and leaves the MFA challenge usable. Boundary: recovery code completes login, consumes once, the same code is rejected on reuse with invalid-code feedback, and an expired pending MFA session plus arbitrary/nonexistent pending-cookie values reject without minting a replacement cookie. Permission: pending token cannot call admin APIs; expired and unknown pending cookies become fully unauthorized. Performance: MFA form responds quickly. Mobile: at 390x844, the MFA challenge panel stays viewport-contained without horizontal overflow, the code input and Verify action remain visible, wrong-code feedback remains contained, and valid TOTP completes login.
Current Status: TOTP MFA login, mobile MFA challenge layout, empty-code required-field validation, wrong-code feedback, pending-cookie API denial, expired pending-session rejection, unknown pending-cookie rejection, recovery-code login burn, and recovery-code reuse rejection regressions passed 2026-06-23

User Story: As a signed-in admin, I want to log out so my current session is revoked and the admin app returns to login.
Expected Behaviour: POST /logout revokes current session row, clears cookie, and unauthenticated /admin routes show login/setup state.
Edge Cases: Already-revoked session behaves as unauthenticated; logout from stale tab should not crash; shared E2E storage state can be invalidated.
Validation Rules: Route requires current session when present; state-changing origin gate applies; cookie clearing uses the admin cookie path.
Known Assumptions: Browser tests using shared storage state must isolate logout specs.
Test Cases: Happy: account menu logout returns to login. Error: stale cookie redirects cleanly. Boundary: repeated logout. Invalid: wrong method returns 405. Permission: unauthenticated user cannot remain in admin. Performance: no spinner loop. Mobile: account menu logout reachable.

User Story: As a site editor, I want to add visible text to the homepage so I can author the first public page content through the visual editor.
Expected Behaviour: The site editor opens the homepage canvas, the module inserter can add a base.text node, the Properties panel updates the selected text prop, the canvas renders the authored text immediately, and Save Draft persists the changed page tree through the CMS APIs.
Edge Cases: Module inserter unavailable; no selected text node after insertion; property panel disabled for insufficient capabilities; blank text values; save failure after visible canvas update; session expiry while editing.
Validation Rules: The page tree mutation must produce a valid base.text node; property updates must validate against module schema; Save Draft must pass site/page validation and capability diff checks before persistence.
Known Assumptions: The automated smoke edits the homepage in a disposable Playwright database; deeper module-picker variants, permission personas, and mobile insertion affordances are tracked by builder/capability rows.
Test Cases: Happy: insert Text and verify canvas text. Error: save/API failure shows feedback through shared save flow. Boundary: blank/default text. Invalid: malformed node props rejected by validation. Permission: site.content.edit/site.structure.edit gates apply by persona. Performance: canvas update visible promptly. Mobile: broader editor mobile insertion remains separate coverage.
Current Status: Focused core owner lifecycle Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery

User Story: As a site editor, I want saved draft edits to survive a browser reload so I can trust that work persisted before publishing.
Expected Behaviour: After Save Draft completes, reloading the admin page reboots the editor, reloads the CMS draft site/page state, and shows the previously authored homepage or selected page text on the canvas after the page is reopened from the Site panel. On a 390px viewport the editor chrome keeps the publish actions reachable without document-level horizontal overflow.
Edge Cases: Reload during a pending save; stale active page id; session expiry; saved site document with missing node references; local UI state reset after reload; cache showing stale draft; narrow toolbar action overflow.
Validation Rules: Save responses and reload fetches are TypeBox-validated; persisted site/page documents pass validateSite/page-tree checks; editor readiness waits for the canvas before assertions; mobile checks assert viewport width/height, document overflow, and publish action containment.

Known Assumptions: The smoke covers explicit save followed by a full browser reload; non-home page reloads reopen the saved page from the Site panel before asserting canvas content; unsaved autosave timing and crash recovery are tracked by reliability rows.
Test Cases: Happy: save text edit, reload, and verify text remains. Error: missing draft after reload. Boundary: reload after exactly one saved module edit; non-home page is reopened from the Site panel after reload. Invalid: corrupted persisted page rejected by validation. Permission: authenticated editor session required. Performance: editor ready within E2E timeout. Mobile: save an isolated page, switch to 390x844, reload, reopen the saved page, verify the saved text, assert no document-level horizontal overflow, and assert the publish actions trigger is reachable.

User Story: As a publisher, I want to publish the current homepage draft so the latest saved site content becomes visitor-visible.
Expected Behaviour: The Publish button triggers the normal publish flow, completes required step-up when needed, calls the CMS publish endpoint, builds publish artifacts/runtime assets, updates publish status, and shows Published feedback in the toolbar.
Edge Cases: No saved draft changes; invalid site document; step-up cancel/wrong password; duplicate publish clicks; publish artifact write failure; stale publish status after success.
Validation Rules: Publish requires pages.publish and fresh step-up as implemented; publish responses are validated; toolbar waits for explicit Published feedback before public assertions.
Known Assumptions: The core smoke uses owner credentials and the default step-up helper; scheduled publish and heavy publish performance are tracked by separate rows.
Test Cases: Happy: saved homepage draft publishes and toolbar reaches Published. Error: publish failure surfaces error state. Boundary: no-op publish after a saved draft. Invalid: invalid site doc blocks publish. Permission: publisher capability plus step-up. Performance: publish completes within E2E timeout. Mobile: publish control reachability tracked separately.

User Story: As a site visitor, I want the published homepage to render without admin chrome so I can read the site as the public audience.
Expected Behaviour: After publish, a fresh anonymous browser context opens the public root route, sees the published homepage text, does not see draft-only text, and does not see admin/editor chrome.
Edge Cases: Missing baked artifact; stale public cache; published CSS/runtime asset failure; admin cookies leaking into public check; draft text leakage; route fallback to admin shell.
Validation Rules: Public visitor helper uses a separate anonymous context; public route resolution serves published output only; public assertions require visible text and absence of editor chrome/draft-only content.

User Story: As a publisher, I want unpublished draft changes to stay private so visitors keep seeing the last published version until I publish again.
Expected Behaviour: After a page is published, editing the same homepage text and saving the draft without publishing updates the editor canvas/draft state but does not alter the anonymous public route, which still renders the last published text.
Edge Cases: Draft save accidentally bumps publish version; public render reads draft tables; dynamic cache uses stale or wrong version; visitor route caches draft-only content; publish status copy ambiguous after post-publish draft save.
Validation Rules: Draft save and publish are separate operations; public route reads active published snapshots/artifacts; public helper asserts published text visible and draft-only text absent after the later draft save.
Dependencies: tests/e2e/core-owner-lifecycle.e2e.ts; tests/e2e/helpers/public.ts; server/publish/publishState.ts; server/publish/renderCache.ts; server/publish/publicRouter.ts; server/handlers/cms/site.ts
Known Assumptions: The smoke uses one text node on the homepage; more complex data-row/template draft isolation is tracked by content/public rows.
Test Cases: Happy: save post-publish draft and verify public still shows prior text. Error: draft-only text appears publicly. Boundary: same node edited after publish. Invalid: stale public cache invalidation mismatch. Permission: editor can save draft without publishing. Performance: public revisit stays prompt. Mobile: public mobile tracked separately.

User Story: As an admin user, I want to see and revoke active devices so I can control account access.
Expected Behaviour: Account Active devices tab lists sessions; DELETE /auth/sessions/:id revokes one; POST /auth/logout-all revokes other sessions and keeps current session.
Edge Cases: Current session revoke through another tab causes redirect; logout-all excludes current session; unknown session id is handled; device labels may be unknown.
Validation Rules: Session routes require authenticated user; params are route-decoded; only self sessions are listed/revoked; revocation actions are step-up gated.
Known Assumptions: Multiple browser contexts simulate other owner sessions; setup project's stored session may also appear and is safe to revoke in disposable E2E.
Test Cases: Happy: list current and other sessions, sign out everywhere else through step-up, keep current session, and force the other context back to login on next request. Error: revoke unknown session shows recoverable error. Boundary: only one current session. Invalid: malformed id. Permission: cannot revoke another user's sessions. Performance: list loads with skeleton. Mobile: Active devices table stays contained in its own horizontal scroller at 390px and the page itself does not overflow sideways.
Current Status: Active-device list and sign-out-everywhere-else Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; individual revoke, current-session rejection, cross-user guard, unknown-session error, and only-current logout-all server regressions passed through 2026-06-23; mobile active-devices table Playwright coverage passed 2026-06-23

User Story: As an authenticated admin, I want sensitive actions to require fresh password/MFA confirmation so risky operations are protected.
Expected Behaviour: Sensitive routes return step_up_required when the session lacks a fresh step-up window; StepUp dialog POSTs /auth/step-up, retries the original action, and stores stepUpExpiresAt.
Edge Cases: Cancel is silent/no mutation; wrong password shows dialog error; MFA-required users may need code; expired step-up forces re-prompt.
Validation Rules: Step-up body requires password and optional mfaCode; server policy has auth mode and window minutes; sensitive handlers call capability plus step-up gates.
Known Assumptions: Publish, import, schema deletes, profile changes, plugin actions are representative sensitive flows.
Test Cases: Happy: correct password retries action. Error: wrong password keeps dialog open. Boundary: prompt reappears after expiry. Invalid: empty password. Permission: lacking capability still blocked after step-up. Performance: retry does not double-submit. Mobile: dialog fields visible.

User Story: As an admin user, I want failed sign-in attempts recorded and abusive attempts rate-limited/locked so suspicious activity is visible.
Expected Behaviour: Failed attempts are stored in login_attempts; lockout/rate-limit states affect login; Account activity tab lists recent successes/failures with suspicious banner for lock/rate-limited events.
Edge Cases: Unknown user attempts still log no_user; event timestamps parse to local display; failed count uses last 24 hours; rate limits should clear after window.
Validation Rules: Result enum includes success, bad_password, no_user, account_disabled, locked, rate_limited, mfa_failed; activity route returns current user's relevant rows.
Known Assumptions: Tests should avoid locking shared owner unless disposable DB is reset.
Test Cases: Happy: failed then successful login appears in history. Error: lockout blocks login with clear error. Boundary: 24h threshold. Invalid: malformed activity request. Permission: only own account history on Account tab. Performance: activity table loads quickly. Mobile: rows do not overflow.
Current Status: Activity-tab failed/successful login Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; locked and rate-limited suspicious-banner component coverage passed 2026-06-23; disposable-account lockout/rate-limit browser regression passed 2026-06-23
Notes: DEF-20260622-013 resolved: successful step-up attempts consumed the blanket per-IP login limiter but did not clear it, so legitimate bulk protected actions from one address could later block unrelated logins with "Too many login attempts from this address." Successful login, MFA verify, and step-up now reset the per-IP limiter. Focused account E2E passed 5/5 including setup. User Story: As an admin user, I want to edit my display name and email so my profile stays current.
Expected Behaviour: Account Profile tab shows current identity; PATCH /me updates displayName/email after step-up and refreshes session user; role information is read-only.
Edge Cases: Duplicate email or invalid email fails; cancellation leaves data unchanged; changing shared E2E email can break later tests.
Validation Rules: Profile body requires displayName max 160 and email length 3-320; server owns current user; step-up is required.
Known Assumptions: E2E should prefer display-name changes unless it can rotate login credentials safely.
Test Cases: Happy: display name saves and persists. Error: duplicate email. Boundary: max display name. Invalid: empty/invalid email. Permission: every authenticated user edits self only. Performance: save feedback clears. Mobile: form usable.
Current Status: Display-name update through step-up Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; PATCH /me step-up denial, trimmed email save, max display-name boundary, invalid profile payloads, and duplicate-email rejection server regressions passed 2026-06-23; mobile Profile form and browser step-up cancel/no-op regressions passed 2026-06-23; browser email rotation remains API-covered future expansion

User Story: As an admin user, I want to upload or clear my avatar so account menus show the intended image.
Expected Behaviour: POST /me/avatar accepts an image upload, creates/associates media, returns avatar URL; DELETE /me/avatar clears avatar and falls back to generated/gravatar display.
Edge Cases: Unsupported MIME fails; large upload should fail cleanly; removing missing avatar is idempotent; media storage errors surface.
Validation Rules: Avatar route accepts FormData; upload validation and media storage rules apply; self-targeted authenticated route.
Known Assumptions: Requires local upload directory or configured media adapter.
Test Cases: Happy: upload image changes avatar. Error: unsupported file shows validation. Boundary: small valid PNG. Invalid: empty form. Permission: user can only update self. Performance: upload progress/feedback. Mobile: upload control reachable.

User Story: As an admin user, I want to change my password so old credentials stop working.
Expected Behaviour: Security tab calls PATCH /me/password through step-up; server hashes new password, rotates session state as implemented, and returns updated user/password timestamp.
Edge Cases: Too-short password rejected; mismatched confirmation is local-only; cancel/no step-up leaves old password; existing sessions are revoked by server policy; autofill should not corrupt fields.
Validation Rules: New password minLength 12; confirmation must match before submit; step-up gate; current user required.
Known Assumptions: Browser E2E must use disposable credentials and restore the shared owner password before the suite continues.
Test Cases: Happy: change password through step-up, reject old password, log in with new password, restore original password. Error: too-short password and mismatched confirmation show field-local alerts. Boundary: exactly 12 characters reaches mismatch validation instead of length validation. Invalid: empty field. Permission: self only. Performance: save state visible. Mobile: Password card action and Change password dialog stay contained at 390px, and fields, footer actions, and validation alerts remain reachable without page-level horizontal overflow.
Current Status: Password-change validation and rotation Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; mobile password card/dialog layout and exact-minimum local validation Playwright coverage passed 2026-06-23; multi-session revocation browser proof and autofill variants remain future expansion
Notes: ACCOUNT-003 promoted in `account.e2e.ts`: the rotation test opens Account Security, verifies too-short password and mismatched confirmation alerts in the Change password dialog, changes the owner password through the shared step-up prompt, verifies the success status, signs out, confirms the old password is rejected, signs in with the temporary password, restores the original shared E2E password through a second step-up, and signs in again with the original password. The mobile ACCOUNT-003 regression opens Account -> Security at 390x844, verifies the Password card action stays contained, opens Change password, verifies fields and footer actions are visible, verifies too-short validation, verifies an exact-12-character mismatch reaches the mismatch alert instead of the length alert, verifies dialog controls and alerts are contained within the viewport, and cancels without changing credentials. User Story: As an admin user, I want to enable/disable MFA and regenerate recovery codes so account security can be managed.
Expected Behaviour: Security tab starts TOTP setup, shows secret/QR, enables with code, stores recovery codes, can disable TOTP, and regenerate codes through step-up-gated endpoints.
Edge Cases: Invalid TOTP code fails; QR rendering failure falls back to secret; recovery-code dialog must be dismissible; canceling setup must not enable MFA.
Validation Rules: TOTP start returns secret and otpauthUrl; enable body requires secret min 16 and code min 6; recovery codes returned once; step-up for sensitive operations.
Known Assumptions: Browser E2E generates TOTP codes from the displayed setup key; invalid setup-code feedback, empty/wrong MFA login-code feedback, mobile MFA login-challenge containment, recovery-code login burn, and reuse rejection are covered by account E2E, and QR-failure fallback is covered by focused AccountPage regression.
Test Cases: Happy: start MFA setup through step-up, verify QR/manual key/authentication-code UI, cancel, reload, confirm MFA remains off, enable MFA with a generated TOTP code, verify recovery codes are shown, sign out/in through MFA, regenerate recovery codes through MFA step-up, disable MFA, and confirm recovery-code generation is disabled. Error: wrong setup code shows the Invalid authentication code inline alert, keeps the setup dialog open, re-enables submit, and leaves MFA Off after cancel/reload; empty MFA login code focuses the required field and exposes native validation before any request; wrong MFA login code shows the same inline alert and leaves the MFA challenge visible until a valid TOTP is submitted; QR rendering failure shows QR code unavailable, keeps the manual setup key and authenticator link visible, and still allows MFA enablement. Boundary: recovery code login consumes one code and rejects reuse. Invalid: malformed secret. Permission: self only. Performance: QR generation failure does not block manual setup. Mobile: setup dialog opens after step-up at 390px with QR/manual key/copy link/code/footer actions contained, the code input is scroll-reachable, entering six digits enables the submit action, and cancel keeps MFA off; MFA login challenge at 390x844 stays viewport-contained with the code input, Verify action, and wrong-code feedback contained before valid TOTP completes login.
Current Status: MFA setup/cancel, invalid setup-code feedback, empty/wrong MFA login-code feedback, QR-failure manual-key fallback, full enable/TOTP-login/recovery-regenerate/disable, recovery-code login burn/reuse rejection, mobile setup-dialog containment, and mobile MFA login-challenge regressions passed through 2026-06-23

ACCOUNT-004 promoted in `account.e2e.ts`: the test enables MFA with a generated code from the displayed manual setup key, verifies the recovery-code dialog shows 10 codes, signs out, clicks Verify with the MFA login field empty and verifies browser required-field validation, submits a guaranteed-wrong MFA login code and verifies the inline `Invalid authentication code` alert without leaving the challenge, completes MFA login with a generated code, regenerates recovery codes through the shared step-up dialog requiring password plus MFA code, disables MFA, and verifies recovery-code generation returns to disabled. User Story: As an admin user, I want to configure my step-up prompt behaviour so sensitive actions match my security preference.
Expected Behaviour: Security card updates mode and window minutes via PATCH /me/security/step-up and returns the updated current user; the route always requires a fresh step-up even when normal sensitive gates are disabled.
Edge Cases: Unsupported mode/minutes rejected; disabled mode bypasses normal user-policy gates but not the policy editor itself; setting a short window should re-prompt soon; cancel leaves current policy.
Validation Rules: mode union is required or disabled; windowMinutes union is 5, 15, 30, or 60; self-targeted route; update is authenticated and step-up-gated with policy always.
Known Assumptions: Browser E2E restores the owner window to the default 15 minutes so later account/security scenarios keep the expected shared state.
Test Cases: Happy: change policy window to 30 minutes through step-up, verify success status and card copy, reload, verify persistence, restore to 15 minutes. Error: API failure shown. Boundary: 5 and 60 minute options are reachable in the window menu, and the server computes the configured expiry timestamp when step-up is reopened. Invalid: unsupported mode/window values reject without changing the stored policy. Permission: self only and policy changes still require step-up when normal gates are disabled. Performance: immediate feedback. Mobile: Step-up authentication controls stack and stay contained at 390px, the Step-up window dropdown stays inside the viewport, and selecting the current value closes without triggering step-up.
Current Status: Step-up window change and reload persistence Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; mobile step-up control/dropdown layout Playwright coverage passed 2026-06-23; disabled-mode bypass, invalid option rejection, and configured expiry timestamp server coverage passed 2026-06-23; browser API failure display remains future expansion; stale-window re-prompt is covered by CAP-003
Notes: ACCOUNT-005 promoted in `account.e2e.ts`: the persistence test signs in fresh, opens Account -> Security, verifies the default required 15-minute step-up state, changes the Step-up window combobox to 30 minutes, completes the shared step-up prompt, verifies the success message and security-card status, reloads and verifies the 30-minute value persists, then restores the window to 15 minutes and verifies the restoration feedback. The mobile ACCOUNT-005 regression opens Account -> Security at 390x844, verifies the required 15-minute policy state, verifies the Step-up authentication controls are stacked and contained, opens the Step-up window listbox, verifies the 5-minute and 60-minute options are reachable, verifies the dropdown stays inside the viewport without page-level sideways overflow, selects the already-current 15-minute option, and verifies no step-up dialog opens because no mutation was requested. Server ACCOUNT-005 coverage in `accountSecurity.test.ts` verifies the policy route requires step-up, disabled mode bypasses normal sensitive gates without bypassing the policy editor, unsupported `mode`/`windowMinutes` values return `Invalid step-up settings` after step-up, invalid payloads leave the stored `required`/15-minute policy unchanged, and a configured 30-minute policy drives the next step-up expiry timestamp. `authStepUp.test.ts` also verifies sensitive actions reject once a fresh step-up window expires; CAP-003 browser coverage now verifies the UI re-prompts before mutation after the current session's `step_up_expires_at` is expired. Initial focused run exposed a test-only interaction bug: the project Select primitive renders a combobox/listbox, not a native select, so the persistence test now clicks the combobox and chooses visible options by role. Mobile focused debugging 2026-06-23 exposed two test-only assertion issues: the first helper compared switch/select left edges too strictly despite screenshot evidence of a stacked contained layout, and Playwright fuzzy matching let `5 minutes` match `15 minutes`; the helper now asserts vertical stacking/containment and option locators use exact accessible names. User Story: As a visitor to /admin, I want the app to choose setup, login, or authenticated admin state correctly.
Expected Behaviour: Static admin shell embeds boot probes for setup status, me, and public site; unauthenticated users see login/setup; authenticated users load lazy workspace shell.
Edge Cases: Expired cookie; public-site fetch failure; direct /admin/unknown routes; Vite dev asset failures; fallback accessible message.
Validation Rules: Boot response schemas validated by client; session cookie scoped to /admin; internal router owns admin navigation.
Known Assumptions: Tests can start from direct URLs with clean/stale cookies.
Test Cases: Happy: /admin loads correct phase. Error: expired session returns login. Boundary: direct /admin/dashboard. Invalid: unknown route fallback. Permission: disallowed workspace redirects/denies. Performance: no long blank screen. Mobile: skeleton/login fit.
Current Status: Manual core lifecycle passed 2026-06-22; automated/build/lint/bundle/Playwright E2E passed; broader exploratory pending

User Story: As an admin user, I want navigation to show only permitted workspaces and direct URLs to respect permissions.
Expected Behaviour: AuthenticatedAdmin maps path prefixes to workspace pages, checks canAccessWorkspace, redirects to first accessible workspace or shows access unavailable.
Edge Cases: Role with no dashboard; plugin page routes; account accessible to every authenticated user; stale nav state after role change.
Validation Rules: Capabilities in src/admin/access.ts drive client gates; server route capabilities must also deny direct API access.
Known Assumptions: Capability tests may need custom roles and separate sessions.

Edge Cases: No active live path falls back to root; content selected entry publishes a posts route; leaving content clears the active live target; publish unavailable outside allowed contexts; account menu stays reachable; toolbar trailer stays contained at phone width.
Validation Rules: Interactive controls use UI primitives; admin links use in-house router; publish actions are capability and step-up gated.
Known Assumptions: Site/content pages update active live path; other workspaces fall back.

Error: logout failure handled lower-level. Boundary: plugin page active nav and missing active live path root fallback. Invalid: missing public site path falls back to root. Permission: publish hidden/disabled without caps. Performance: toolbar should not import editor store outside site. Mobile: toolbar text/buttons do not overlap.

User Story: As an admin user, I want global settings for site metadata, publishing, preferences, and shortcuts so I can adjust editor behaviour.
Expected Behaviour: Settings opens from the global toolbar on authenticated admin routes, renders General, Shortcuts, Publishing, and Preferences sections, loads site-level settings on non-editor routes, writes editor preferences to localStorage through catalog-derived Switch/Select controls, restores those values after reload, ignores malformed stored preferences by falling back to defaults, and remains contained on 390px viewports.
Edge Cases: Missing preference storage; malformed JSON in localStorage; non-editor dashboard route with no editor store; Select menu focus handoff before Escape; narrow phone viewport; unavailable site settings request; shortcut list is read-only.
Validation Rules: Editor preference reads parse localStorage with the TypeBox-derived EditorPrefsSchema and catalog defaults; preference writes use setEditorPreference/setEditorSelectPreference and notify subscribers; site settings load/save through useSiteSettingsController and CMS validation; controls use shared Button/Switch/Select primitives.
Known Assumptions: Editor preferences are intentionally local to the browser/device and do not mutate the site file; the Playwright test restores any pre-existing localStorage value after the run.
Test Cases: Happy: open Settings from Dashboard, verify General/Shortcuts/Publishing/Preferences sections, toggle Auto-save, change Auto-save delay and UI density, reload and verify persistence. Error: unavailable site settings should surface via the existing Settings error path. Boundary: empty or corrupt localStorage falls back to Auto-save on, 30-second delay, Compact density. Invalid: malformed stored preference JSON is ignored. Permission: site-level saves require site capabilities, local preferences are client-owned. Performance: modal opens from Dashboard without loading the editor. Mobile: 390x844 Settings Preferences controls and active section tab remain viewport-contained without document-level horizontal overflow.

Feature Name: Workspace layout, panels, toasts, and error recovery
User Story: As an admin user, I want workspace panels, resizers, notifications, and error boundaries to recover predictably so normal work is not derailed by layout or async failures.
Expected Behaviour: Workspace layouts persist sidebar/panel state, floating panels remember position, toast bus renders transient messages, confirm dialogs replace native dialogs, and error boundaries isolate page/workspace failures where installed.
Edge Cases: Corrupted localStorage; panel closed then route change; async component throw; toast after unmount; stale toast remaining in the bus; narrow viewport resizing.
Validation Rules: Layout/preference stores parse with fallbacks; shared dialogs/toasts use UI primitives and role alert/status; toast bus producers and tests import through the Toast barrel; error-boundary coverage is gated by architecture tests.
Known Assumptions: This cross-cutting row covers UX shell reliability rather than one route.
Test Cases: Happy: resize/close panels and reload; publish success/error notifications announce through ToastProvider. Error: injected component/load failure shows boundary and root callbacks log. Boundary: corrupted layout storage falls back, stale toast can be dismissed, and no provider test leaks notifications between runs. Invalid: native dialogs absent. Permission: same as owning workspace. Performance: layout changes do not thrash. Mobile: panels do not overlap content.
Current Status: ADMIN-005 focused shell-reliability Bun regressions, content workspace Playwright panel regression, and Browser mobile smoke check passed 2026-06-23 after DEF-20260623-ADMIN005-01, DEF-20260623-ADMIN005-02, and DEF-20260623-ADMIN005-03; dashboard/media/plugin/admin-navigation E2E provide route-level layout/toast evidence; injected route-failure exploratory review remains future coverage
Notes: DEF-20260623-ADMIN005-01: Reproduction: write a ToastProvider regression importing dismissToast from `@ui/components/Toast`, the canonical Toast barrel. Expected result: toast producers/tests can import the bus dismiss API through the barrel because the bus contract says producers can dismiss imperatively. Actual result: Bun reported `Export named 'dismissToast' not found`. Severity: P3 developer-facing API consistency defect with stale-toast cleanup risk. Root cause hypothesis: Toast index exported `pushToast` but omitted the matching `dismissToast` bus function. Fix: export `dismissToast` from src/ui/components/Toast/index.ts and keep the test on the barrel import. DEF-20260623-ADMIN005-02: Reproduction: persist the Content left sidebar at 520px, reload the Content workspace at 390x844, and inspect the Content panel close affordance. Expected result: the left panel rail and close button remain viewport-contained without document-level horizontal overflow. Actual result: the close button rendered beyond the 390px viewport before the fix. Severity: P2 mobile workflow defect because users could lose the close affordance after desktop resizing. Root cause hypothesis: LeftSidebar.module.css applied the persisted desktop panel width directly to the rendered flex width on phone viewports. Fix: cap the rendered left sidebar/panel width with CSS variables based on 100vw while preserving the stored layout width for desktop restoration. Verification: focused `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/admin-navigation.e2e.ts -g ADMIN-005` passed 2/2 including setup. DEF-20260623-ADMIN005-03: Reproduction: persist Content left sidebar width 520px and right settings width 300px, reload the Content workspace at 390x844 with both sidebars open, and inspect the Settings panel close affordance. Expected result: the settings close button remains viewport-contained and reachable. Actual result: before the fix, the button right edge rendered at 684px in a 390px viewport. Severity: P2 mobile workflow defect because users could lose the settings-panel close affordance after desktop resizing. Root cause hypothesis: RightSidebar.module.css kept the right sidebar as an in-flow flex item after the left panel/canvas on phone viewports, so even a capped panel width could be laid out offscreen. Fix: cap the right sidebar rendered width and, at phone widths, anchor the right sidebar absolutely to the workspace stage right edge while preserving the stored layout width for desktop restoration. Verification: red focused ADMIN-005 E2E failed on the settings close button at x=684; green focused ADMIN-005 E2E passed 2/2 including setup after the fix, including click-through from Settings close to Content close; Browser mobile smoke at 390x844 reported both close controls ending at x=384, zero document overflow, no console warnings/errors, and successful Settings close recovery. src/__tests__/layout/workspaceLayoutStorage.test.ts verifies per-workspace layout storage, floating panel positions, partial merge behavior, v2 storage shape, corrupt JSON fallback, and admin-path workspace mapping. src/__tests__/ui/toastProvider.test.tsx verifies ToastProvider renders error and warning toasts with role alert/assertive aria-live, success and info toasts with role status/polite aria-live, surfaces title/body/location/kind metadata, and dismisses a visible toast through the Button close affordance. src/__tests__/dialogs/confirmDeleteProvider.test.tsx verifies destructive actions can require an app alertdialog before committing. src/__tests__/architecture/no-native-browser-dialogs.test.ts rejects production alert/confirm/prompt usage. src/__tests__/architecture/error-boundary-coverage.test.ts verifies required ErrorBoundary seams, unique location tags, React 19 root callbacks, canonical primitive location, shared logging helpers, and single ToastProvider mounting. Verification: focused `bun test src/__tests__/ui/toastProvider.test.tsx` passed 2/2 with 18 assertions; focused `bun test src/__tests__/ui/toastProvider.test.tsx src/__tests__/layout/workspaceLayoutStorage.test.ts src/__tests__/dialogs/confirmDeleteProvider.test.tsx src/__tests__/architecture/error-boundary-coverage.test.ts src/__tests__/architecture/no-native-browser-dialogs.test.ts` passed 19/19; TSV 14-field check and `git diff --check` passed; `bun run lint` passed; `bun run build` passed; full `bun test` passed 5703/5703 with existing React act warnings and expected export/plugin scheduler error logs.

Feature Name: Workspace isolation and limited admin navigation
User Story: As an owner or admin, I want limited users to reach only the admin workspaces their role grants so protected workspace data and actions stay hidden.
Expected Behaviour: Custom roles expose workspaces through capability checks in src/admin/access.ts. A user with site.read and media.read can reach Site, Media, and self-targeted Account; disallowed workspace links are hidden and direct disallowed admin routes redirect away without rendering protected screens or data. At 390px, the limited toolbar remains contained, the granted Media affordance and account trigger remain reachable, and denied Content/Users affordances remain absent.
Edge Cases: Users with no dashboard access fall through to the first accessible workspace; Account remains available to every authenticated user; role changes can leave stale client navigation until the current user refreshes; direct denied URLs must not flash protected content; background plugin work must not run without plugin workspace access; narrow toolbar must not reintroduce hidden denied workspaces or overflow.
Validation Rules: Workspace visibility is derived from canAccessWorkspace and firstAccessibleWorkspace; role capabilities are normalized by the Users role editor and enforced again by server route capability gates; denied direct navigation must not render protected headings or tables; mobile checks assert viewport size, document overflow, toolbar containment, granted Media link containment, and account trigger containment.
Known Assumptions: The Playwright scenarios use disposable owner sessions to create View site + Browse media roles and users; Account reachability is code-defined self-service behavior; mobile assertions target the shared toolbar containment for restricted workspace sets.
Test Cases: Happy: limited Site + Media user logs in and sees Media while staying out of Users/Content. Error: direct denied /admin/users navigation redirects away. Boundary: no dashboard capability falls through to Site. Invalid: stale or unknown workspace path must not expose protected UI. Permission: server APIs still require their own capabilities. Performance: no background plugin fetch/SSE for non-plugin roles. Mobile: limited user signs in at 390x844, sees Site and Media without denied Content/Users affordances, toolbar has no document-level horizontal overflow, Media and account controls remain contained, and Account menu opens with Account & security reachable.
Current Status: Focused CAP-001 desktop and mobile Playwright regressions passed 2026-06-23
Notes: Verification: `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/users.e2e.ts -g "CAP-001"` passed 3/3 including setup. The desktop scenario creates a custom View site + Browse media role, creates a limited user, signs in from a clean context, verifies Media is visible while Content and Users are absent, then opens `/admin/users` directly and verifies the route redirects away and the All Users heading is not rendered. The mobile scenario creates the same restricted persona, signs in at 390x844, verifies Site and Media are present while Content/Users are absent, verifies the toolbar and account trigger are viewport-contained without page-level horizontal overflow, opens Account menu, and verifies Account & security remains reachable. Related access rules live in `src/admin/access.ts`; Account remains self-targeted for every authenticated user by `canAccessWorkspace`. Run logs: docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-cap001-workspace-isolation.md; docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-cap001-mobile-limited-navigation.md.

User Story: As an admin user, I want a dashboard with site metrics and quick status widgets so I can understand CMS state.
Expected Behaviour: /admin/dashboard renders the dashboard page with greeting/actions, onboarding facts, Overview header, Today/7d/30d/All range state, Customize/Add block controls, and the default nine first-party widgets: storage, pages, posts, media, status, activity, publish lineup, plugins, and domain. Dynamic widgets fetch their own dashboard endpoint and leave aria-busy once loaded; status and domain render static operational rows; the mobile Overview header stacks/wraps controls.
Edge Cases: Empty site and zero counts; per-widget API failure leaves that widget loading instead of crashing the dashboard; missing plugin widget definitions render skeleton placeholders; no saved layout preference falls back to the default layout; first-party range tabs are local state today; narrow viewports can overflow if header controls do not wrap.
Validation Rules: Dashboard workspace requires dashboard.read through admin access gates; widget endpoints require authenticated user or domain capability (media.read, plugins.read, audit.read); client responses are TypeBox-validated through apiRequest; dashboard layout preferences are schema-validated and recovered from invalid stored values.
Known Assumptions: The default E2E owner has dashboard, media, plugin, and audit capabilities; E2E storage label is SQLite; status/domain widget values are currently static placeholders; plugin-contributed widgets are outside the default clean-install grid.
Test Cases: Happy: owner opens dashboard and sees all default first-party widgets with loaded data. Error: widget endpoint failure should not blank the page. Boundary: clean install with zero media/posts/plugins. Invalid: invalid saved layout falls back. Permission: limited users without widget capabilities do not receive protected widget data. Performance: widgets load progressively. Mobile: at 390px the Overview header, range tabs, Customize, and Add block controls fit without horizontal overflow.

User Story: As a new admin, I want onboarding tasks that reflect setup progress so I know what to do next.
Expected Behaviour: /admin/dashboard defers onboarding rendering until setup facts load. When dashboard-layout onboardingDismissed is false, OnboardingPanel renders five tasks from useOnboardingState: identity (site name changed from Untitled Site or favicon), framework (site.settings.framework), first page (site.pages.length >= 2), plugin (installed plugin count), and team (users.length > 1). Clean E2E state shows 2 of 5 steps complete because site identity and the seeded first page are complete; framework is in progress and plugin/team are not started. Task actions either open Settings or route to /admin/site, /admin/plugins, and /admin/users. Dismiss saves dashboard-layout with onboardingDismissed true and reload keeps the panel hidden. At 390px the panel and Add members action stay contained.
Edge Cases: Facts endpoints can fail individually and Promise.allSettled falls back to defaults instead of bricking the dashboard; all tasks may be complete; dismissed panel stays hidden; corrupted dashboard-layout preference falls back through layout recovery; generated posts entry template makes firstPage complete in clean E2E; target workspaces may still be access-gated by route capabilities.
Validation Rules: Client API responses are validated through cmsAdapter, listCmsPlugins, and listCmsUsers; dashboard-layout preference schema includes onboardingDismissed boolean; dashboard route requires dashboard.read; target routes enforce their workspace capabilities.
Known Assumptions: Clean E2E site name is Automated E2E Site; the posts entry template is seeded so firstPage is complete; owner has settings, site, plugins, and users access; no plugins or extra team members exist in clean E2E state.
Test Cases: Happy: verify 2/5 progress, five task labels/states/actions, Settings modal action, workspace routes, server-backed dismiss save, and reload-hidden persistence. Error: facts API soft failure is covered by implementation inspection; focused browser regression does not stub each failed endpoint. Boundary: dismissed panel remains absent after reload. Invalid: corrupted preference fallback remains lower-level/manual. Permission: target routes remain behind workspace gates. Performance: panel waits for facts before rendering. Mobile: at 390px the panel and Add members action stay contained.

Feature Name: Visual editor canvas selection and rendering

Expected Behaviour: Canvas renders active page/VC/template in iframe frames, overlays selection/hover/ladder, suppresses public form controls, and syncs selected node to panels.
Edge Cases: Missing module definition; locked/hidden nodes; iframe load race; node inside visual component slot; canvas zoom/pan.
Validation Rules: Renderer validates node props through module engine; canvas queries only iframe documents; readonly regions enforced.
Known Assumptions: Browser evidence needed for overlay geometry and iframe behaviour.
Test Cases: Happy: select visible node and panel updates. Error: missing module placeholder. Boundary: hidden/locked nodes. Invalid: bad node props fallback. Permission: read-only can select but not edit. Performance: large tree still responsive. Mobile: canvas controls visible.

Inserted modules are added through the active tree insertion target and selected in the editor.

Error: search no matches shows empty state in lower-level coverage. Invalid: hidden internal modules and malformed saved layout refs cannot be picked. Permission: structure edit required to mutate the active tree. Performance: search/filter and recent rendering complete within E2E timeout. Drag: drag Text from the filtered picker into the center of an existing Container, verify the inside drop preview, edit the inserted Text, and verify it renders inside that Container. User Story: As a site editor, I want undo and redo so I can safely reverse edits without losing the current saved draft.
Expected Behaviour: Loading or creating a site resets transient history and renders Undo/Redo unavailable. Undoable site mutations commit Mutative patch pairs scoped to the SiteDocument, expose Undo, and clear Redo. Undo applies the inverse patches, moves the entry to the future stack, marks affected document paths dirty, and enables Redo. Redo applies forward patches and clears itself when no more future entries remain. A new mutation after Undo clears the future branch. Save/reload persists the saved site tree but clears transient undo/redo stacks. Keyboard shortcuts use the keybinding registry and are ignored inside inputs/textareas/contenteditable surfaces.
Edge Cases: Empty history; redo after a new edit; save/reload boundary; page switches after history entries; coalesced same-prop typing bursts; non-coalescing mutations ending a burst; visual-component and runtime mutations; no-op mutations; maximum history eviction; stale activePageId after undo; Windows/Linux Ctrl+Y redo alias.
Validation Rules: All tree/site mutations route through centralized history helpers; no-op recipes return false and must not push history; loadSite/createSite/clearSite clear stacks and flags; UndoRedoButtons keeps unavailable controls rendered with aria-disabled true, delegates canonical shortcuts to the registry, and guards editable targets.
Known Assumptions: History is client-side editor state, not a persisted audit log; saved draft content survives reload, but undo/redo availability intentionally does not; enabled Button controls omit aria-disabled rather than rendering aria-disabled=false.
Test Cases: Happy: loaded page starts with disabled history controls, insert Text, undo by Ctrl/Cmd+Z, redo by Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Z, undo by button, then insert Container and verify Redo clears. Error: empty-history Undo/Redo stay unavailable and no-op lower-level. Boundary: same-prop edits coalesce into one undo entry; history caps at MAX_HISTORY; save/reload preserves saved content and resets history controls. Invalid: no-op mutations and stale ids do not create dirty history. Permission: history actions depend on prior allowed editor mutations. Performance: patch history applies O(change). Mobile: keyboard shortcuts may be unavailable; phone-width visual reachability remains future coverage.
Current Status: SITE-009/BUILDER-005 expanded Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; focused store and toolbar/keybinding tests cover coalescing, branch clearing, no-op, history cap, shortcut registry, and editable-target guards; no product defects found in this slice; phone-width notch reachability and VC-mode browser permutations remain residual

Feature Name: Framework colors, typography, spacing, and fonts
User Story: As a site editor, I want reusable color tokens, font tokens, typography scales, spacing scales, and generated locked utility classes so the site design remains consistent across canvas and published pages.
Expected Behaviour: ColorsPanel writes framework color tokens and generated locked color classes; TypographyPanel and SpacingPanel write scale groups, manual/fluid settings, class-generator rows, and font tokens; buildFrameworkPlan emits one merged :root block plus generated utility classes; generated framework CSS is tree-shaken by default but can emit all utilities; font routes list bundled Google fonts, estimate/install Google selections, register custom media-backed fonts, and delete on-disk font families; font tokens emit CSS variables and update exact var(--font-*) references when renamed.
Edge Cases: Duplicate or colliding token slugs are deduped; generated class name collisions claim/remap user classes; orphan framework-prefixed classes are pruned; disabling utilities removes assigned classes; invalid color values are sanitized at CSS emission; rootFontSize cannot be below 1; unsafe font paths, unicode-range injection, wrong media MIME, empty family, empty variants/subsets, duplicate font token variables, and deleting a referenced font family are blocked or filtered.
Validation Rules: Framework and font settings are TypeBox-derived schemas; color slugs and font token variables normalize before storage; framework CSS values are sanitized before emission; font file paths must be /uploads/* or media-backed https; custom font variants parse before registration; all font endpoints require site.style.edit; admin mutation CSRF origin gate applies.

User Story: As a site editor, I want to create, preview, configure, edit, save, and publish site files so custom CSS, JavaScript, images, and binary assets can participate in the authored site without leaving the visual editor.
Expected Behaviour: CodeEditorPanel opens the active site file, lazy-loads CodeMirror for text/script/style/node-prop buffers, renders image previews for image files, renders a non-image binary placeholder for other binary assets, and exposes ScriptSettingsPane or StyleSettingsPane for runtime files. Site Explorer creates stylesheet files under src/styles/<name>.css and script files under src/scripts/<name>.ts, sets the new file active, and file mutations persist through site.files. Script settings control enabled state, module/classic format, canvas execution, placement, timing, priority, import diagnostics, and page/template scope. Stylesheet settings control enabled state, priority, and page/template scope. Deleting a runtime file clears its runtime config and active editor selection. Publisher emits enabled scoped user stylesheets into page-specific userStyles bundles and enabled scripts into published runtime assets.
Edge Cases: Empty text files remain editable and saveable; image files preview by URL while other binary files show a placeholder; invalid or unsafe paths are rejected; duplicate normalized paths are rejected; deleting an active file closes the editor; deleting scripts/styles removes runtime config; disabled or out-of-scope styles/scripts do not publish for the page; script import diagnostics do not run for classic scripts; CodeMirror content syncs through a short debounce before save.
Validation Rules: SiteFileSchema and SiteFileType validate persisted file shape; filesSlice normalizes paths, rejects traversal/empty/colliding paths, and routes content/blob updates by file kind; SiteRuntime schemas normalize script/style defaults and scope targeting; collectRuntimeScripts and collectAppliedStyles filter by enabled state, page/template scope, priority, and path order; collectUserStylesheetCss comment-wraps source paths and feeds the userStyles CSS bundle; public CSS links are emitted only for non-empty bundles.
Known Assumptions: CodeMirror is intentionally lazy-loaded; browser specs wait for the CodeMirror content element before typing; direct script authoring plus dependency resolution is tracked in SITE-014, while SITE-013 owns the generic file editor and stylesheet publish path; local E2E uses disposable SQLite data and can publish public pages without external services.
Test Cases: Happy: create stylesheet from Site Explorer, type CSS in CodeMirror, save draft, publish, verify public page loads one /_instatic/css/userStyles-* link and applies the CSS. Error: invalid/duplicate/unsafe paths reject in files data-layer tests; resolve/import errors remain surfaced by the script settings/dependencies flows. Boundary: empty files, image preview, non-image binary placeholder, disabled runtime config, style/script priority ordering, page/template scope, and delete cleanup. Invalid: path traversal and unsafe normalized paths are rejected. Permission/security: runtime endpoints and site save/publish remain capability/step-up gated by surrounding flows; published output omits admin chrome and serves immutable hashed CSS. Performance: CodeMirror loads lazily and userStyles hashing changes only when relevant stylesheet content/config changes. Mobile/responsive: dependency/code-authoring mobile reachability is covered in SITE-014; broader standalone code-editor mobile exploration remains a residual manual check.
Current Status: SITE-013 automated audit passed 2026-06-23 with direct stylesheet browser coverage; no open defects; broader standalone mobile exploratory remains pending

Feature Name: Site dependencies and runtime package resolution
User Story: As a site editor, I want to declare dependencies so plugin modules and site code can use runtime packages.
Expected Behaviour: Dependencies panel edits package metadata; POST /runtime/dependencies/resolve installs/resolves package import map; published pages serve cached runtime packages under /_instatic/runtime/cache.
Edge Cases: Invalid package.json; network/install failure; stale hash; package not found; runtime path 404 under namespace.
Validation Rules: Resolve body accepts unknown packageJson and normalizes to safe runtime dependencies only; devDependencies are not resolved into runtime importmaps; client validates dependencyLock/packageImportmap envelopes; runtime package paths require a 24-hex hash and reject traversal.
Known Assumptions: Package installs may need network; deterministic tests use mocked registry/install/cache roots and UI fetch stubs.
Test Cases: Happy: dependency panel detects imports, adds missing dependency, resolves lock/importmap, preview/build consumes runtime deps, package server serves cached package assets, public importmap points at hashed cache URLs, browser can load the emitted package asset, and mobile code authoring exposes the missing-dependency Add action without horizontal overflow. Error: resolve API failure surfaces in store; network/install timeout/cap/partial cache handled; missing package asset 404. Boundary: no dependencies, stale lock, importmap missing, concurrent resolves, cache sentinel, relative RUNTIME_CACHE_DIR. Invalid: unsafe package names, malformed lock envelope, bad runtime hash, traversal path. Permission: runtime.dependencies/site.read caps. Performance: install cache reuse and concurrent install dedupe. Mobile: Code Editor authoring and dependency panel controls stay reachable at 390px.
Current Status: Passed 2026-06-23: deterministic SITE-014 coverage (75 tests), cache layout regression (8 tests), Site Explorer layering invariant (33 tests), focused browser E2E (3 tests including setup), full bun test (5695 pass), lint, and build.

Feature Name: Site editor media explorer and picker
User Story: As a site editor, I want to browse, upload, reuse, inspect, edit, and apply media from inside the site editor so image, video, SVG, and background/media controls can use CMS assets without leaving the authoring workflow.
Expected Behaviour: MediaLibraryControl renders library and URL modes for image/video props, lazy-loads MediaPickerModal on Browse, filters by media kind, updates the prop with the picked asset publicPath, supports clearing, and opens MediaViewerWindow for the selected CMS asset. MediaExplorerPanel is a docked left-rail panel that lists CMS assets grouped as Images, Videos, and Other, supports search and list/grid view persistence, uploads assets through CMS media APIs, opens the shared viewer, exposes context menu actions for Copy URL, Rename, Delete, and conditionally Use in selected image/video when the selected canvas node matches the asset kind. Published pages render selected local media through /uploads URLs.
Edge Cases: Empty library shows bucket empty states; image/video/other assets are bucketed by MIME type; selected image/video actions only appear for matching module and asset kind; unsupported uploads return an alert through the media upload queue; deleted or missing selected paths fall back to saved-path labels; URL mode accepts local /uploads paths plus http/https URLs and rejects invalid image/video URLs; SVG uploads are sanitized before serving; viewer edits/removal update local asset state; upload queue and media viewer can be closed without leaving the editor.
Validation Rules: CMS media responses are validated by @core/persistence/cmsMedia; uploads are server magic-byte/type/size checked and routed through the media presentation pipeline; MediaLibraryControl validates URL mode before calling onChange; MediaExplorerPanel applies assets only to selected base.image src or base.video videoUrl props; media routes require the relevant media capabilities; publisher escapes/render-validates media URLs and visitor pages must not include admin chrome.
Known Assumptions: The docked Media Explorer and the property-control picker intentionally share CMS media and viewer primitives with the Media workspace; direct background-image picker publishing remains covered by lower-level style/publisher tests rather than a dedicated browser journey; the new SITE-015 browser regression uses disposable SQLite/uploads and verifies public media output as a visitor.
Test Cases: Happy: upload an image through the property picker, select it for an image module, save, publish, and verify public /uploads image decoding; reuse the same library asset on a second image without re-upload; upload an image through the docked Media Explorer, use its context menu to apply it to the selected image module, save, publish, and verify public /uploads image decoding. Error: unsupported upload shows specific rejection feedback; media API errors surface inline or restore optimistic state. Boundary: empty library, search filters, list/grid view, image/video/other grouping, selected image/video context action gating, metadata rename/edit/reload persistence, replace/delete/restore/purge lifecycle, and sanitized SVG serving. Invalid: bad URL-mode values are rejected before prop update; unsafe SVG script/event/style content is stripped. Permission/security: media APIs require media capabilities and public visitor pages show only uploaded media, not admin chrome. Performance: MediaPickerModal lazy-loads only after Browse; media panel fetches assets when opened. Mobile/responsive: media viewer, replace dialog, trash restore, and storage panel have mobile containment coverage; docked editor Media Explorer mobile remains a residual exploratory check.

User Story: As a site editor, I want to preview the current draft and open the live route so I can compare draft and published output.
Expected Behaviour: Preview page is exposed from the publish-actions menu; PreviewOverlay mounts only when previewOpen, requires an active site and active page, renders publishPage(activePage, site, registry) into a sandboxed iframe srcDoc, shows the active page title, closes by Close button/Escape/backdrop, and restores focus. OpenLivePageButton is globally mounted in the toolbar, reads adminUi.activeLivePath, and opens that path in a new noopener/noreferrer tab or falls back to the site root. useActiveLivePath publishes regular page paths, template preview targets, post-type preview permalinks, or /404 for not-found templates.
Edge Cases: No active site/page renders no overlay; unpublished saved drafts can preview without changing public output; live route shows last published artefact until publish; activeLivePath null opens root; template pages are not directly routable and resolve to their preview target; popup uses the current dev/admin origin but Vite proxies public routes; narrow viewport must keep preview reachable and document width contained.
Validation Rules: Preview state is owned by uiSlice openPreview/closePreview; overlay generation trusts the validated in-memory site document and the publisher boundary sanitizes emitted HTML; iframe uses sandbox="" with no allow flags; OpenLivePageButton receives the already-resolved public path from adminUi; save/publish remain capability and step-up gated by surrounding toolbar flows.
Known Assumptions: Public live routes intentionally show the last published version, not the saved draft; this slice covers regular pages, while template/content-entry live-path permutations remain covered lower-level or future browser coverage.
Test Cases: Happy: create page, publish version A, save draft version B, open Preview page and verify iframe shows B, open live page and verify popup route shows A without admin chrome. Error: no-site/no-active-page overlay and close behaviours covered by component tests. Boundary: activeLivePath root fallback, home path, content entry path, template target resolver, and mobile 390px preview reachability. Invalid: missing runtime preview body remains covered by server runtime tests, not this client overlay. Permission/security: publish/save capability and step-up gates surround the flow; public popup has no admin chrome. Performance: preview lazy-loads publisher graph only when opened. Mobile: overlay opens at 390px without document overflow.
Current Status: SITE-016 automated browser audit passed 2026-06-23 with draft-vs-live comparison and mobile preview reachability; no open defects; template/content live-path browser permutations remain residual risk

User Story: As a site editor, I want to componentize authored page content, add reusable slots, fill those slots on a page, and publish the resulting page as clean visitor HTML.
Expected Behaviour: Componentize converts the selected page node into a Visual Component row with a base.body definition root, replaces the original page node with a base.visual-component-ref, and switches the editor into VC mode. Adding a base.slot-outlet to the VC definition creates a locked base.slot-instance child on the page ref when returning to the page. The locked slot row hides destructive structural actions but accepts inserted child content. Save writes component rows before page rows so newly-created component refs validate; publish inlines the component definition and slot fill into the public artefact without editor slot labels or component names.
Edge Cases: Duplicate or blank component names; conversion attempted from VC mode, body/root, or an existing component ref; recursive refs; unknown or missing componentId; slot outlet rename/reorder/delete; empty or default slots; nested refs; page save racing a new component save; save/publish/reload after slot fill insertion.
Validation Rules: Component names and recursion use typed VisualComponent errors; VC/page shapes are TypeBox-validated through component/page adapters; syncSlotInstances materializes locked slot instances from slot outlets; dirty tracking marks both edited page and created component; CmsAdapter writes components before pages; publisher renderVisualComponentRef resolves refs, expands slot-instance children at matching outlets, and sanitizes emitted HTML.
Dependencies: src/admin/pages/site/panels/PropertiesPanel/ConvertToComponentButton.tsx; src/admin/pages/site/store/slices/visualComponentsSlice.ts; src/admin/pages/site/store/slices/site/dirtyTracking.ts; src/core/persistence/cms.ts; server/handlers/cms/pages.ts; server/handlers/cms/components.ts; src/core/visualComponents; src/modules/base/visualComponentRef; src/modules/base/slotOutlet; src/modules/base/slotInstance; src/core/publisher/renderVisualComponentRef.ts
Known Assumptions: Visual Components persist as rows in the components system table; page rows can reference a component only after that component row is stored; E2E uses disposable local SQLite/uploads and fresh owner login because publish rotates the session.
Test Cases: Happy: componentize Text, create a slot outlet, return to page, insert Text into the locked slot, save, publish, and verify anonymous public page contains component body plus slot fill. Error: adapter ordering regression prevented new component refs from validating during page save. Boundary: locked slot row remains operable while hiding Rename/Duplicate/Cut/Delete; empty/default/nested/unknown component behavior covered by lower-level suites. Invalid: blank/duplicate names and recursive refs rejected by focused tests. Permission: publish step-up exercised; fine-grained capability variants remain lower-level/future browser coverage. Performance: save ordering is sequential only where required, layouts remain independent. Mobile: VC publish journey desktop-covered; mobile VC editing remains residual.

User Story: As a site editor/content author, I want page templates and bindings so data rows render through site pages.
Expected Behaviour: Site Explorer creates template pages through Template settings, stores enabled template target and priority on the page row, opens the template in canvas mode, and shows synthetic preview data for postType templates. DynamicBindingControl auto-scopes string props on postTypes templates to the targeted table, inserts `{currentEntry.title}` tokens into text props, and `base.outlet` implicitly binds currentEntry.body. Save Draft persists template state, Publish snapshots it, and public `/posts/:slug` routes render the highest-priority matching published template with the published row title and body.
Edge Cases: No matching template or row; seeded default template tie/order; deleted target table/field; no compatible fields; empty/null title/body values; unpublished template or row; duplicate template slug; invalid binding path; missing outlet; loops nested inside templates.
Validation Rules: TemplateSettingsDialog requires nonblank title, unique normalized slug, numeric priority, and at least one selected post type when target kind is postTypes. PageTemplateConfig is TypeBox-parsed from page rows; binding picker filters fields by control compatibility; token interpolation and dynamic prop resolution tolerate unknown/empty fields; `base.outlet` body HTML is sanitized at the publisher boundary; public entry routes use published rows and published site snapshots only.

Known Assumptions: Seeded Posts and its default entry template exist in disposable E2E state; the SITE-018 browser regression creates a higher-priority Posts template so it wins deterministically; content row publishing is step-up gated and runs from a fresh session.
Test Cases: Happy: create a Posts template, bind title/body, save/publish, publish a post, and verify public route. Error: no matching row/template falls back or 404s in lower-level route tests. Boundary: seeded default template loses to higher priority; template preview uses synthetic Example Post data before real rows exist. User Story: As a site editor, I want to save and reuse layouts so common structures can be inserted quickly.
Expected Behaviour: Layouts are stored in the layouts system table; the Save as layout dialog rejects blank and duplicate names; the module inserter lists saved layouts under Layouts; inserting a saved layout clones the captured subtree and style rules with fresh node ids; renaming or deleting the saved layout does not affect already-inserted page content.
Edge Cases: Duplicate layout names; blank names; saved layout source on page root; stale selections after page creation; mobile/narrow inserter category labels; context-menu z-index inside the spotlight inserter; deleted modules/VC refs inside a layout; invalid tree; deleting a saved layout already used by a page has no page effect.
Validation Rules: Layout route validates layout document; clone remaps node ids/scoped classes; system table locked from rename/delete; addPage clears stale canvas selection; module inserter category buttons keep explicit accessible names when labels hide responsively; saved-layout manage menu renders above the spotlight layer.
Known Assumptions: Layouts are not separate DB tables; selector selection can persist across page switches, but stale node selection must not.
Test Cases: Happy: save styled container subtree as layout, insert it on a new page, save/reload/publish, verify public output. Error: blank and duplicate names render inline errors. Boundary: narrow 390px inserter exposes Layouts. Invalid: stale node selection is cleared on addPage. Permission/security: structure edit and system table gates remain server-covered. Performance: picker remains responsive with saved item. Manage: rename and delete saved layout; inserted content persists.

User Story: As a publisher, I want to schedule the active page or post row for future publication so content can go live later without an immediate publish click.
Expected Behaviour: Site toolbar scheduling opens SchedulePublishDialog for the active page or post row, captures a local future datetime, posts the ISO time to the row schedule endpoint, marks the row scheduled, surfaces scheduled pages in the dashboard Pages count and Publish lineup, and tickPublishScheduler publishes due rows as a system actor.
Edge Cases: Past datetime; local timezone conversion; close/cancel without mutation; multiple scheduled rows; server restart; advisory lock duplicate prevention; due row already changed before tick.
Validation Rules: Dialog requires a date/time value and blocks past times; RowScheduleBodySchema requires scheduledPublishAt; handler requires publisher permission and canPublishDataRow; repository lists due scheduled rows; scheduler uses advisory locking and a system publisher.
Known Assumptions: Site scheduling targets the active page row rather than a whole-site static publish; the editor Page shape does not currently prefill scheduledPublishAt in the dialog; dashboard widgets are the user-visible scheduled status surface.
Test Cases: Happy: schedule active page from Site toolbar and verify dashboard scheduled count plus Publish lineup route. Error: past time is rejected by dialog/server. Boundary: local datetime serializes to ISO near timezone boundaries. Invalid: missing datetime disables scheduling. Permission/security: publisher permission and data-row publish gate. Performance: dashboard lineup refreshes from summary endpoint. Mobile: dialog and publish menu remain reachable on narrow viewport.
Current Status: Focused PUBLISH-002 page scheduling Playwright regression and scheduler tick Bun regression passed 2026-06-23

User Story: As an operator, I want publish state and cache invalidation to be correct so visitors do not see stale or mixed pages.
Expected Behaviour: Static artifacts serve disk-first from uploads/published/current, render cache keys include publishVersion, version bump evicts dynamic cache, and status endpoint reports current publish state.
Edge Cases: Partial artifact slot; stale CSS hash; dynamic route cache after publish; unpublish artifact removal.
