Feature Name: Row status, author, table move, and preview APIs
User Story: As a data/content operator, I want row-level status, author, table move, and preview operations so rows can flow through publishing and templates.
Expected Behaviour: Rows endpoints publish/schedule/delete schedule/set status/author/move table/preview return updated row or preview payload, with authorization by ownership/capability. Schedule rejects past times, schedule/cancel/status responses update Content UI state immediately where used, status retractions clear publish and schedule metadata, cancel only applies to scheduled rows, status only retracts to draft/unpublished while publish uses /publish, author changes require active users, table move rejects target slug collisions, and Content collection moves surface only when data.rows.move is granted.
Edge Cases: Moving between incompatible tables; target table already has row slug; schedule delete when not scheduled; past schedule times; scheduled row retracted through status; unsupported status values; blank or suspended author; non-postType preview; preview invalid cells; publish failure; edit-only content user attempting table move.
Validation Rules: Route table decodes row id; status schema union; schedule body validates { at } then future time; status mutation clears `published_at`, `published_by_user_id`, and `scheduled_publish_at`; author body requires a nonblank active user; move requires data.rows.move on server, rejects missing target tables and non-deleted target slug conflicts, and uses canMoveDataRow in Content UI; publish own/any checks; preview validates body and postType table kind before rendering.
Known Assumptions: Some operations appear in Content and Data workspaces; schedule/status browser coverage exercises the Content publishing toolbar and settings state while lifecycle, metadata cleanup, preview, author, and conflict edge coverage runs at the CMS handler/API layer.
Test Cases: Happy: publish, schedule future publish, immediately display scheduled row state, cancel schedule, unpublish, retract scheduled row to draft, and permitted content table move. Error: target slug conflict returns 409 and leaves the source row in its original table. Boundary: scheduled status, cancel when not scheduled, unsupported published status through /status, edit-only user without move controls. Invalid: past schedule time, blank/suspended author, non-postType preview. Permission: own/any publish/edit, author-manager, and move caps. Performance: preview quick. Mobile: row lifecycle controls remain broader UI coverage.
Current Status: Passed 2026-06-23: DATA-004 browser schedule/status regression passed, full content E2E passed, row lifecycle plus scheduled-metadata cleanup API regressions passed, row-move conflict API regression passed, content row table-move capability regression passed, full capabilities E2E passed, and repository build/test/lint gates passed.
Notes: DEF-20260623-004 closed: focused Content schedule E2E failed red because the schedule dialog closed after a successful schedule but the row still rendered `draft`; SchedulePublishDialog now passes the returned DataRow to ContentToolbar/ContentPage and the selected entry/list/settings state updates immediately. DEF-20260623-005 closed: focused API regression failed red because `/status` changed a scheduled row to `draft` while retaining `scheduledPublishAt`; updateDataRowStatus now clears `scheduled_publish_at` with publish metadata. Verification: focused schedule E2E passed 2/2 including setup; full `tests/e2e/content.e2e.ts` passed 9/9; `bun test src/__tests__/server/cmsDataAuthorization.test.ts` passed 9/9 with 127 expects; TSV shape/duplicate guards and `git diff --check` passed; `bun run build` passed; full `bun test` passed 5692/5692 with 135723 expects; `bun run lint` passed. Prior DATA-004/CAP-004 proofs remain: row lifecycle, target slug-conflict move, and content row-move capability regressions passed. User Story: As a media manager, I want to upload files with feedback so assets are available to content and site editors.
Expected Behaviour: MediaPage/controls POST uploads through XHR queue, validate MIME/size/storage plan, auto-open queue, create media records, and show progress/success/error.
Edge Cases: Unsupported type; duplicate filename; huge file; upload cancel/failure; storage adapter external upload step failure.
Validation Rules: Upload route/form validates media MIME and body; client XHR path validates response schema; media.write required.
Known Assumptions: Local uploads dir must be disposable for E2E.
Test Cases: Happy: upload PNG and see thumbnail. Error: unsupported file message. Boundary: small/large allowed sizes. Invalid: empty upload. Permission: no media.write hides upload. Performance: progress updates. Mobile: upload control usable.
Current Status: MEDIA-001..003 and CAP-004 media read/write Playwright coverage passed 2026-06-22; published uploaded-media browser response/decode regression passed 2026-06-23.
Notes: DEF-20260622-008 resolved: media.read-only users no longer see upload or internal move affordances, while media.write users can upload. CAP-004 Playwright regression verifies Upload media is absent for a reader and visible/functional for a writer; existing media.e2e.ts upload, unsupported upload, and library reuse regressions still pass. 2026-06-23 MEDIA-001/PUBLIC-003 browser pass verifies a published page image sourced from `/uploads/` is visible, complete, has nonzero natural width, and loads with a 200 `image/png` browser response. Verification: `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/capabilities.e2e.ts -g "CAP-004"`, `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/media.e2e.ts`, full `tests/e2e/capabilities.e2e.ts`, and focused `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/media.e2e.ts -g "MEDIA-001"`. User Story: As an admin user, I want to browse/search media assets so I can find and reuse files.
Expected Behaviour: MediaCanvas shows grid/list, smart folders/tags/type buckets, selection/multi-select, viewer window, and picker modal reuse in other workspaces.
Edge Cases: Empty library; deleted/soft-deleted asset; stale thumbnail; many assets; keyboard selection.
Validation Rules: Media list requires media.read; client schemas validate media assets; smart folder logic buckets by MIME/path.
Known Assumptions: Media picker may filter images/videos/files.
Test Cases: Happy: browse/search/select asset. Error: list load failure. Boundary: zero assets. Invalid: stale selected id. Permission: no media.read denies workspace. Performance: many assets usable. Mobile: grid/list fits.
Current Status: Automated baseline, build, lint, bundle, and Playwright E2E passed; manual exploratory pending

User Story: As a media manager, I want folders so assets can be organized.
Expected Behaviour: Folder sidebar lists/create/rename/delete folders; asset folder assignment endpoint adds/removes memberships; folder tree hydrates asset counts.
Edge Cases: Deleting non-empty folder; duplicate name/path; moving asset between folders; folder id missing; smart folders vs user folders.
Validation Rules: Folder routes require media.write/manage as implemented; folder body validated; asset assignment body has add/remove arrays.
Known Assumptions: Exact folder delete semantics are repository-owned.
Test Cases: Happy: create folder and assign asset. Error: duplicate folder. Boundary: empty folder delete. Invalid: bad folder id. Permission: media.write/manage split. Performance: count hydration quick. Mobile: folder tree usable.
Current Status: Automated baseline, build, lint, bundle, and Playwright E2E passed; manual exploratory pending

User Story: As a media manager, I want to rename and tag media assets so assets have useful labels and alt text.
Expected Behaviour: Viewer and bulk edit update filename, altText, caption, title, tags via PATCH /media/:id and refresh selection/grid.
Edge Cases: Invalid filename/path; concurrent rename; bulk edit partial failure; tags empty/duplicates; title too long.
Validation Rules: Update body allows optional filename, altText, caption, title, tags array; server validates asset existence and capabilities.
Known Assumptions: Metadata affects editor/public media rendering where used; viewer field saves are debounce-backed but blur flushes immediately.
Test Cases: Happy: upload image, open viewer, edit title/filename/alt/caption/tags, reload, verify persistence. Error: invalid filename. Boundary: empty metadata. Invalid: tags wrong type. Permission: media.write required. Performance: debounce saves without blocking typing. Mobile: at 390px, Uploads close, viewer containment, Replace, Title, Filename, Alt text, tag entry, and metadata PATCH saves remain reachable without horizontal overflow.
Current Status: MEDIA-004 viewer metadata persistence and mobile viewer layout Playwright regressions passed 2026-06-23; invalid filename handling, bulk-edit partial failure, empty metadata boundaries, and permission-gate variants remain future expansion
Notes: MEDIA-004 promoted in `media.e2e.ts`: the desktop persistence test signs in fresh, uploads a disposable PNG from the Media workspace, opens the asset viewer, updates Title, Filename, Alt text, and Caption through the viewer fields, adds a tag with Enter, verifies the renamed asset tile and viewer state, reloads `/admin/media`, reopens the renamed asset, and verifies all metadata persists. Feature Name: Media replace, delete, restore, and purge
User Story: As a media manager, I want to replace or remove assets safely so media library stays accurate.
Expected Behaviour: POST /media/:id/replace overwrites bytes and updates the visible asset filename to the uploaded replacement name, DELETE soft-deletes active assets or purges trashed assets, POST /restore restores soft-deleted assets, and viewer/grid/trash actions reflect capabilities.
Edge Cases: Replacing with wrong type; deleting asset used on published page; purge force; restore after purge; storage delete failure; upload queue overlay after upload; mobile context menu overlap with expanded sidebars.
Validation Rules: replace requires media.replace; delete/purge requires media.delete; upload validation applies to replacement; route params decoded; Trash folder distinguishes restore vs purge actions.
Known Assumptions: Public pages may retain published references until republish/asset unavailable handling; replacement keeps one asset record but adopts replacement filename.
Test Cases: Happy: upload image, replace it, verify viewer reflects replacement, soft-delete from All files, restore from Trash, delete again, purge from Trash. Error: unsupported replacement. Boundary: restore soft-deleted asset. Invalid: missing file. Permission: replace/delete caps separate. Performance: replacement progress. Mobile: at 390px, Replace file dialog, media context-menu Delete, Trash navigation, and Restore remain usable without horizontal overflow.
Current Status: MEDIA-005 replace/delete/restore/purge Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; mobile replace/trash-restore and context-menu hit-test regression passed 2026-06-23; unsupported replacement, asset-in-use/public reference behavior, confirmation/safety review, and permission variants remain future expansion
Notes: MEDIA-005 promoted in `media.e2e.ts`: the desktop test signs in fresh, uploads disposable PNG, opens viewer, replaces file through Replace file dialog with a second PNG, verifies replacement filename and Replaced detail, closes viewer, deletes active asset from Media item options, verifies it leaves All files, opens Trash, restores it, verifies it leaves Trash and returns to All files, deletes it again, opens Trash, purges it from Media item options, and verifies Trash is empty. The mobile regression switches to 390x844, clears stored layout, uploads a disposable PNG, closes the Uploads floating window, opens the viewer, verifies mobile containment, opens Replace file, verifies the shared dialog is contained and the replacement picker is reachable, replaces the binary, verifies the replaced viewer remains contained, closes it, opens the item options menu by keyboard, deletes the asset, opens Trash, restores it, returns to All files, and verifies no page-level horizontal overflow. DEF-20260623-MEDIA005-01 closed: the point-anchored shared ContextMenu rendered inside the media canvas with viewport coordinates, so at 390px its clamped menu overlapped the expanded Folders sidebar while the sidebar intercepted pointer events; fixed by portaling the shared ContextMenu to document.body and covering that contract in `contextMenu.test.tsx`. Verification: focused `bun test src/__tests__/ui/contextMenu.test.tsx` passed 6/6 after first failing on the portal regression; focused `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/media.e2e.ts -g "keeps replace and trash restore"` passed 2/2 including setup; full `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/media.e2e.ts` passed 10/10 including setup. Run logs: docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-22-media005-replace-delete-restore.md; docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-media005-mobile-lifecycle.md.

User Story: As an operator, I want to choose storage backends and understand migration state so media storage can move safely.
Expected Behaviour: Media Storage panel loads from GET /media/storage, lists each media asset role with its elected adapter, defaults every role to Local disk (built-in), shows Local sharp ladder (built-in) when no variant delegate is elected, shows empty adapter/delegate states when no plugin storage surfaces are installed, exposes external adapter verification only for external adapters, and shows migration controls only when original/variant backlog exists.
Edge Cases: Adapter unavailable; migration crash/retry; tab close cancel; partial migration; invalid delegate; no plugin adapters installed; no migration backlog on local-disk install; mobile panel overflow or clipped close controls.
Validation Rules: Elect body role original/variant/avatar/font/plugin-pack and adapterId; delegate body string/null; migration body role and toAdapterId; storage.elect gates panel/list/elect/delegate/verify; storage.migrate gates migration stream.
Known Assumptions: External adapters and delegates require plugin fixtures; local disk exists by default and clean installs have no migration backlog.
Test Cases: Happy: open Storage panel and verify local disk/local sharp defaults plus empty adapter/delegate states. Error: external verify failure. Boundary: no backlog hides migration. Invalid: unknown adapter/delegate. Permission: storage.elect/storage.migrate caps. Performance: storage snapshot loads quickly. Mobile: at 390px, storage panel opens, every built-in role combobox is reachable, delegate and empty adapter states can be scrolled into view, close control works, and no horizontal overflow appears.
Current Status: MEDIA-006 built-in storage panel Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; mobile storage panel layout regression passed 2026-06-23; external adapter verify/elect, migration backlog/progress, delegate plugin election, and storage.migrate permission variants remain future expansion

User Story: As a visitor/admin, I want media variants and public URLs to serve correctly so images load efficiently.
Expected Behaviour: Image variant worker/protocol produces raster variants where applicable, media presentation maps asset public paths and variant paths for editor/published use, signed/storage URLs are redirected by public media routing, and SVG uploads are sanitized before bytes are stored and served.
Edge Cases: Variant worker failure; SVG unsafe content; original missing; external signed URL expiry; media batch resolution; raster too small for variants; SVG made empty by sanitizer.
Validation Rules: Variant protocol validates sizes/ids; SVG sanitizer strips script blocks, foreignObject, event handlers, javascript: URLs, and style blocks; public route serves trusted MIME based on server-chosen extension.
Known Assumptions: Some raster variants may be generated lazily or skipped for small images; SVGs intentionally do not get raster variant ladders.
Test Cases: Happy: upload unsafe SVG, verify asset appears, fetch served /uploads SVG, verify safe geometry remains and script/foreignObject/event/javascript/style vectors are stripped. Error: missing variant fallback. Boundary: SVG asset. Invalid: sanitizer-empty SVG rejected. Permission: public read for published assets, admin caps for manage. Performance: variant generation bounded. Mobile: responsive attrs correct.
Current Status: MEDIA-007 unsafe SVG sanitizer/public media Playwright regression passed 2026-06-22; raster variant ladder/srcset, missing variant fallback, external signed URLs, media batch resolution, and responsive attrs remain future expansion
Notes: MEDIA-007 promoted in `media.e2e.ts`: test signs in, uploads an unsafe SVG through the Media workspace, verifies the asset tile appears, reads the tile preview /uploads URL, fetches the served SVG through Playwright's request context, verifies Content-Type is image/svg+xml, verifies safe <rect> geometry remains, and verifies <script>, <foreignObject>, event-handler attributes, javascript: URLs, and <style> blocks are absent from served bytes. Verification: focused `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/media.e2e.ts -g "MEDIA-007"` passed 2/2 including setup; full `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/media.e2e.ts` passed 8/8 including setup. Run log: docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-22-media007-svg-sanitizer.md.

Edge Cases: Cleanup failure prompts remove anyway; disabling plugin removes admin pages/widgets/modules; restart sandbox error; busy state prevents duplicate clicks.
Validation Rules: Lifecycle routes require plugins.lifecycle/configure/install depending action; runtime uses grantedPermissions; removal protects filesystem paths.
Known Assumptions: Plugin runtime errors are observable in UI; parked-error restart needs an error fixture.
Test Cases: Happy: install packaged fixture plugin, disable, verify runtime route unregistered, enable, verify runtime route restored, confirm uninstall, verify card and route are removed. Error: cleanup failure force path. Boundary: already disabled. Invalid: unknown plugin id. Permission: lifecycle/install caps. Performance: no stale cards. Mobile: card actions usable.

User Story: As a plugin author/operator, I want sandboxed server/editor/module code to have only granted permissions so extensions are powerful but contained.
Expected Behaviour: QuickJS-WASM VM runs plugin entrypoints with typed SDK factory, RPC targets enforce grantedPermissions for content/media/network/storage/routes/hooks/crypto/schedules, and outbound hosts are allowlisted.
Edge Cases: Declared but not granted permission; wildcard host; network denied; timeout/deadline; binary IO; content table mode mismatch.

User Story: As an operator, I want invalid plugin uploads to fail with a specific recoverable error so I can correct the package without refreshing or risking a partial install.
Expected Behaviour: Plugins page parses JSON manifests or inspects zip packages before install review; malformed/invalid manifests set the page alert, do not show a permission review, and the next valid upload clears the alert and opens review.
Edge Cases: Malformed JSON; manifest schema failure; invalid zip; incompatible apiVersion; retry with valid package; file input same-name reupload.
Validation Rules: JSON path uses parsePluginManifest; zip path uses inspectCmsPluginPackage; upload control requires plugins.install; install mutation happens only after permission review and step-up.
Known Assumptions: Current browser regression covers invalid JSON manifest recovery; invalid zip/package fixtures remain lower-level until a stable fixture is added.
Test Cases: Happy: invalid manifest shows inline alert, no review is opened, then valid manifest opens a zero-permission review. Error: malformed manifest. Boundary: retry after failure. Invalid: bad plugin id. Permission: installer only sees upload control. Performance: parse failure returns quickly. Mobile: alert/review remain visible.

User Story: As a site editor, I want semantic layout modules so page structure and template outlet content render cleanly.
Expected Behaviour: base.body represents page body and renders children directly while editor identity is applied to the iframe body; base.container renders selected/custom HTML tag; base.outlet renders page/template outlet content or fallback HTML.
Edge Cases: Void custom tag; unsafe custom tag; outlet missing content; body wrapper only editor-side.
Validation Rules: Props schemas validate tags/attrs/html; resolveHtmlTag and htmlAttributes helpers sanitize/escape; body has pass-through props.
Known Assumptions: Published output intentionally has no framework runtime.
Test Cases: Happy: render body/container/outlet publish contracts and insert container/outlet to publish semantic HTML. Error: invalid container/outlet tag fallback. Boundary: void custom container tags and empty outlet content region. Invalid: unsafe attributes filtered. Permission: structure/content edit. Performance: render is pure and simple. Mobile: authored layout depends on CSS.

Feature Name: Text, list, link, button, image, SVG, and video modules
User Story: As a site editor, I want common content/media modules so pages can include text, lists, links, calls to action, images, SVG, and video.
Expected Behaviour: Each base module defines props schema/defaults/editor/render, supports inline text where configured, sanitizes attributes/URLs/media attrs, and emits semantic HTML; base.svg publishes sanitized inline markup with an accessible label when provided.
Edge Cases: External link target rel; missing image src; unsupported video URL; SVG unsafe; SVG title entities; line breaks; list items empty.
Validation Rules: Props schemas validate unions/defaults; safeUrl/mediaAttrs/anchorRel/htmlAttributes helpers escape; SVG sanitizer applies at publisher and editor preview boundaries; plain string props arrive pre-escaped.
Dependencies: src/modules/base/text; src/modules/base/list; src/modules/base/link; src/modules/base/button; src/modules/base/image; src/modules/base/svg; src/modules/base/video; src/__tests__/base-modules.test.ts; src/__tests__/core/sanitizeSvg.test.ts; src/__tests__/htmlImport/svgMapping.test.ts
Known Assumptions: Images/videos usually reference media assets or URLs; inline SVG markup is trusted only after sanitizeSvg.
Test Cases: Happy: render text/list/link/button/image/SVG/video publish contracts and insert/edit each module. Error: missing media placeholder and unsupported video URL. Boundary: empty label/text, transparent SVG image asset, empty inline SVG, and list blank lines. Invalid: javascript URLs and SVG script/event/foreignObject vectors. Permission: content edit for props, structure for insert. Performance: render/cache. Mobile: responsive attrs.
Current Status: MODULE-002 common content/media render-contract Bun coverage passed 2026-06-23; browser insertion/edit/publish permutations, permission variants, media-picker SVG/image/video combinations, and responsive authored-layout review remain exploratory
Notes: DEF-20260623-001 resolved: base.svg double-escaped pre-escaped `title` props when publishing `aria-label`, so a title such as `Logo "mark"` rendered as `Logo &quot;mark&quot;` in the attribute instead of one escaped entity layer. Severity P3 accessibility/content polish. Reproduction: render `SvgModule` through `escapeProps` with inline SVG markup and a quoted title. Expected: `aria-label="Logo "mark""`; actual before fix: `aria-label="Logo &quot;mark&quot;"`. Root cause: `base.svg` called `escapeHtml` inside render even though plain string props are already escaped by `escapeProps`. Fix: export `SvgModule`, add it to base conformance, trust the escaped label at render, and add SVG render/editor sanitizer coverage. Verification: `bun test src/__tests__/base-modules.test.ts src/__tests__/core/sanitizeSvg.test.ts src/__tests__/htmlImport/svgMapping.test.ts` passed 120/120. Run log: docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-module002-common-media-modules.md.

Edge Cases: Duplicate ids/names; required hidden/file inputs; option selected state in multi-select; message formId mismatch; malformed validation pattern; hostile form payloads; canvas native-control activation.
Validation Rules: Each control has propsSchema defaults; module conformance verifies pure render contracts; identifiers are normalized where configured; publisher boundary escapes authored attrs/text; validation rejects unknown/invalid payloads; canvas suppresses native form activation while preserving node selection.
Known Assumptions: Works with FORM-001/002 for CMS-native submissions; formId normalization preserves capitalization while cleaning unsupported characters.
Test Cases: Happy: compose CMS-native controls, derive snapshots, submit to data table, and publish runtime JS. Error: duplicate/missing bindings shown in settings analysis and invalid public submissions rejected. Boundary: empty name falls back to fieldId, disabled/readOnly controls, selected options, formId mismatch. Invalid: unsafe action/redirect URL, escaped attrs/text, invalid regex pattern, unknown payload field. Permission: structure/content edit and public endpoint origin/token checks. Performance: form runtime only included when needed. Mobile: controls accessible.
Current Status: Automated focused and full repo verification passed 2026-06-23: all form modules in module conformance, semantic render contracts, publisher-boundary escaping, configured formId normalization, snapshots/settings analysis, runtime emission/challenge-submit delegation, public endpoint validation/security, canvas suppression, form settings panel, full bun test, lint, and build. Validation Rules: Props schemas require slotName/componentId strings; module conformance covers all three definitions; transparent modules render no standalone output; slotSync materializes/renames/reorders/deletes instances; recursion guard prevents cycles; publisher special renderer inlines VCs and escapes missing IDs.

User Story: As an operator, I want all admin/AI/CMS mutations protected by origin checks, capabilities, and TypeBox validation so unauthorized or malformed requests do not mutate data.
Expected Behaviour: CMS and AI dispatchers reject invalid Origin on state-changing methods, require capabilities per route, parse request bodies with TypeBox, and return { error } envelopes on failure.
Edge Cases: Missing Origin for curl/admin API; dev allowlist; wrong method 405; raw malformed JSON; direct URL/API bypass attempt.
Validation Rules: readValidatedBody is the shared request boundary; requireCapability checks sessions/caps; routeTable returns 404/405 consistently; architecture tests gate handlers.
Known Assumptions: This row covers cross-cutting API security, not one screen.
Test Cases: Happy: authorized valid request mutates. Error: invalid origin 403. Boundary: GET not origin-gated. Invalid: malformed body 400. Permission: missing cap 403. Performance: auth checks cheap. Mobile: N/A.
Current Status: Passed 2026-06-23: route-shape fuzzing across 76 CMS and 11 AI mutation endpoints, deployed-origin/proxy boundary permutations, focused SECURITY-001 bundle (184 tests), full bun test (5686 pass), lint, and build.
Notes: Focused run 2026-06-23 added deployed-origin/proxy boundary coverage; see docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-security001-deployed-origins.md. Route-shape fuzzing log: docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-security001-route-fuzzing.md. Live deployed smoke and browser-observed API error UX remain operator-run.

User Story: As a keyboard-only admin, I want to complete the login screen without a mouse so I can access the CMS through standard form focus and submit behaviour.
Expected Behaviour: The unauthenticated admin shell renders the Admin Login form when setup is complete; the Email input can receive focus, Tab moves to Password, typing credentials fills the fields, and Enter submits the form and lands in the authenticated admin shell.
Edge Cases: Initial boot can show setup instead of login on a clean database; MFA-enabled users enter a second keyboard-only challenge; wrong password clears the password field and leaves the alert visible; rate-limit or lockout responses keep the user unauthenticated.
Validation Rules: Email and password fields are required inputs; login body is TypeBox-validated; POST /admin/api/cms/login normalizes email, verifies password, records activity, and returns either current user or MFA pending state; session cookies are scoped to /admin.
Known Assumptions: Automated coverage starts after the Playwright setup project has created the owner, so it covers keyboard login rather than the first-run setup form.
Test Cases: Happy: focus Email, type owner email, press Tab to Password, type password, press Enter, and verify authenticated admin. Error: wrong password remains on login with an alert. Boundary: setup-complete boot path with anonymous storage. Invalid: missing required fields stay browser-invalid. Permission: unauthenticated only until login succeeds. Performance: login completes within E2E timeout. Mobile: covered by auth/account mobile rows.

User Story: As a site editor, I want a fresh admin editor route to become usable quickly so I do not get stuck on a blank page, spinner, or broken shell after opening the app.
Expected Behaviour: Opening `/admin/site` with an authenticated session loads the Site workspace, shows the toolbar and account controls, renders the canvas root and insert notch, removes the app loading screen, and does not emit console errors during startup within a generous 20 second smoke budget.
Edge Cases: Server still warming; browser cache state; plugin event bridge startup; session redirect; slow machine; Vite dev-server noise; transient network failure.
Validation Rules: Authenticated owner storage state required; `expectEditorReady` requires `canvas-root` and `canvas-notch`; the app loading screen is `role="status"` named `Loading Instatic`; console error capture starts before navigation.
Known Assumptions: Automated smoke is a usability threshold, not a detailed performance profile; deeper timing budgets, CPU throttling, bundle analysis, and repeated cold/warm comparisons remain agent-run.
Test Cases: Happy: open `/admin/site`, reach editor chrome under 20s, no stuck loader, no console errors. Error: console error or timeout fails. Boundary: fresh page with existing owner session. Invalid: unauthenticated redirect covered by auth tests. Permission: owner must have site.read/edit access. Performance: primary target. Mobile: covered elsewhere.

User Story: As a site editor, I want publishing a page with several common modules and media to give clear completion feedback and finish promptly so I can trust the publish workflow under realistic page weight.
Expected Behaviour: A disposable page with text, a linked button, and an uploaded image can be created, saved, published through the normal step-up-gated toolbar flow, reach the `Published` state, and serve the expected text, link, and uploaded image on the anonymous public route within a generous 30 second smoke budget.
Edge Cases: Step-up prompt timing; upload/media processing; publish artefact write latency; stale public route; duplicate publish clicks; slow local filesystem; heavier page shapes than the smoke.
Validation Rules: Owner login required because publishing rotates session through step-up; media upload must pass server magic-byte validation; `publishDraft` waits for Published feedback; public visitor context must see no admin chrome and must load the published route.

Feature Name: Create and open a site page
User Story: As a site editor, I want to create a new page from the Site Explorer so the page becomes available for editing in the visual canvas.
Expected Behaviour: The Site Explorer New page action opens the page creation dialog, accepts a name and slug, creates the page, hides the dialog, shows an Open page tree item with the new name, and selecting that item marks it selected in the editor tree.
Edge Cases: Duplicate slugs; reserved slugs; missing or empty names; stale tree state after dialog close; failed create request; existing homepage collision; page created but not openable.
Validation Rules: The New page dialog uses page slug validation, duplicate-slug checks, shared dialog inputs, and the site/page store creation action; create and reload data flow through validated CMS persistence APIs before the tree item is rendered.
Known Assumptions: The automated browser regression creates uniquely named disposable pages in a local SQLite E2E database; deeper invalid slug and duplicate handling are covered by validation/error rows and lower-level tests.
Test Cases: Happy: create a unique page and open it in the canvas. Error: failed create should leave recoverable dialog feedback. Boundary: unique generated slug. Invalid: reserved/empty/duplicate slug blocked by dialog validation. Permission: requires authenticated Site page creation access. Performance: tree item appears within E2E timeout. Mobile: page creation on narrow editor chrome remains future exploratory coverage.

Feature Name: Switch pages without losing unsaved edits
User Story: As a site editor, I want to switch between pages after making an unsaved edit so I can compare pages without losing in-memory draft work before I save.
Expected Behaviour: After inserting and editing Text on one page, the toolbar/status exposes Unsaved draft, switching to a second page hides that first page text, switching back shows the unsaved text again, and saving plus reloading preserves the text.
Edge Cases: Unsaved edits on multiple pages; save failure after page switching; stale active page after reload; missing selected page tree item; browser reload before save; session expiry; conflicting edits from another tab.
Validation Rules: Editor state keeps page drafts by page id until save; Save Draft persists through validated site/page APIs; canvas assertions read the active page iframe after each page selection.
Known Assumptions: The automated case covers one unsaved text edit across two disposable pages, then explicit save and reload; crash recovery and multi-tab conflict handling remain separate reliability coverage.
Test Cases: Happy: unsaved text survives page switch and persists after save/reload. Error: save failure should not falsely report persistence. Boundary: one page switch away and back. Invalid: corrupted page tree rejected by validation. Permission: authenticated Site edit access required. Performance: page switching and canvas updates complete within E2E timeout. Mobile: narrow editor page switching remains future coverage.

Expected Behaviour: After inserting Text and setting an initial headline, inserting Image moves the Properties panel to the image src control; selecting the Text row from Layers restores text controls, editing text updates the canvas, and the old headline disappears.

User Story: As a site editor, I want visible undo and redo controls plus keyboard shortcuts to move page state backward and forward so I can recover from recent visual edits.
Expected Behaviour: A loaded page with no local history renders both notch controls unavailable. Inserting Text creates one undoable visual edit and leaves Redo unavailable. Ctrl/Cmd+Z removes the Text layer and enables Redo. Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Z restores it. Clicking Undo removes it again. Inserting a different module after that Undo clears Redo and starts a new history branch. Saving and reloading persists the new module but resets transient Undo/Redo availability.
Edge Cases: Undo disabled despite history; enabled control still marked aria-disabled; redo available after a fresh insert; shortcut handler firing inside inputs; redo branch not cleared by a new mutation; saved content lost after reload; stale selected node after undo; property edit bursts not coalesced.
Validation Rules: Undo/redo buttons operate on the editor history store and shared Button primitive; unavailable state is `aria-disabled=true`; enabled state omits aria-disabled; keyboard shortcuts come from `editor.undo`/`editor.redo` registry entries and are ignored inside editable targets; visual assertions use layer rows after each action.
Known Assumptions: The browser regression covers one loaded page, one undo/redo keyboard round trip, one button undo, one branch-clearing edit, and one save/reload reset. Store tests cover deeper coalescing, max-history, no-op, runtime, VC, and dirty-tracking cases.
Test Cases: Happy: insert Text, shortcut undo/redo, button undo, branch with Container, save/reload. Error: controls fail to mutate tree or expose wrong availability. Boundary: clean loaded page and reload boundary. Invalid: empty-history controls stay unavailable; no-op mutations covered lower-level. Permission: edit access required. Performance: tree updates promptly. Mobile: phone-width notch history reachability remains future coverage.

User Story: As a security-conscious administrator, I want sensitive operations to require fresh password confirmation so stale sessions cannot publish, install plugins, import destructively, or perform secondary account changes.
Expected Behaviour: Publish and other sensitive actions open the step-up dialog when freshness is required; cancel or wrong password leaves the resource unchanged; correct password proceeds and the action completes; after a fresh step-up window expires, the next sensitive UI action re-prompts before mutation; at 390px the create-user step-up dialog remains viewport-contained with password, cancel, confirm, and inline error states usable; for MFA-enabled sessions, sensitive-action step-up requires both the password and current TOTP code.
Edge Cases: Side effects before confirmation; wrong-password mutation; wrong-MFA-code mutation; stale freshness after timeout; plugin install/uninstall bypass; destructive import bypass; secondary API actions bypassing browser UI; mobile dialog overflow, clipped controls, or hidden wrong-password feedback.
Validation Rules: Sensitive routes and UI actions require fresh step-up state in addition to capability checks; request bodies and responses use TypeBox validation and explicit error envelopes; MFA-enabled users must include a valid second factor for step-up.
Known Assumptions: Promoted browser coverage spans publish, plugin install/uninstall, destructive import, desktop/mobile/MFA user creation, expired-window re-prompt, and secondary API gates.
Test Cases: Happy: correct password allows sensitive action. Error: cancel, wrong password, and wrong MFA code do not mutate. Boundary: after successful step-up rotates the session, expiring that session's step_up_expires_at in disposable SQLite reopens the step-up dialog before the next create-user mutation. Invalid: bad password or TOTP returns inline failure. Permission: action still requires capability. Performance: dialog resolves within E2E timeout. Mobile: at 390x844, the create-user step-up dialog stays horizontally and vertically contained, required controls are visible, wrong-password feedback stays contained, and correct password creates the user. MFA: after MFA login, create-user step-up renders the second-factor field, keeps confirm disabled until it is filled, rejects a wrong code without creating the user, and creates the user with a valid TOTP.

User Story: As an administrator, I want data and media roles to expose only their permitted browse, mutate, import, export, upload, replace, and delete affordances so operators cannot overreach their role.
Expected Behaviour: Custom personas see and use only the data or media operations granted to them; read-only users can browse without mutate actions; data manager/exporter/importer personas expose only their matching controls; content row move controls require data.rows.move and allowed movers can move entries between collections; media writer can upload/edit metadata without replace/delete; media replacer can replace bytes without metadata/delete; and media deleter can trash/purge without upload/replace.
Edge Cases: Destructive actions exposed too broadly; read-only users seeing write menus; import/export mismatch; content row move implied by edit; media upload without write access; replace/delete implied by media.write; data rows visible to unauthorized users; mixed capabilities across workspaces.
Validation Rules: Role capabilities gate workspace access, menu/actions, upload/import/export/row-move/replace/delete controls, and server handlers; CMS responses are validated by the shared HTTP layer.
Dependencies: tests/e2e/capabilities.e2e.ts; src/admin/pages/data; src/admin/pages/content/ContentPage.tsx; src/admin/pages/content/components/ContentExplorerPanel/ContentExplorerPanel.tsx; src/admin/pages/content/components/ContentSettingsPanel/ContentSettingsPanel.tsx; src/admin/pages/media; server/handlers/cms/data.ts; server/handlers/cms/media.ts; server/repositories/roles.ts
Known Assumptions: The browser regression focuses representative data read/manage/import/export, content row-move, and media read/write/replace/delete affordance splits plus content row-move and media replace/delete happy paths.
Test Cases: Happy: permitted data/media operation is visible and usable, and a row mover can move a content entry between collections. Error: forbidden affordance hidden. Boundary: read-only browse without write menus; edit-only content user has no move controls; media.write does not imply replace/delete. Invalid: ungranted import/export/row-move/replace/delete blocked by UI and server. Permission: custom personas verify role gates. Performance: workspaces load within E2E timeout. Mobile: responsive admin shell covered separately.
Current Status: Passed 2026-06-23: focused CAP-004 content row-move regression and full capabilities E2E passed; CAP-004 split coverage now includes data read/manage/import/export, content row move, and media read/write/replace/delete.

User Story: As an administrator, I want plugin and AI surfaces to honor separate read, install, lifecycle, settings, schedule, pack, chat, provider, audit, and AI write-tool permissions so each role sees only its allowed operational surface.
Expected Behaviour: Plugin reader personas can view without install controls and can inspect schedules without schedule mutation controls; plugin installer personas can reach install/remove/pack re-sync flows without settings/lifecycle controls; plugin configurators can open Settings without install/lifecycle controls; plugin lifecycle operators can see lifecycle and schedule run/pause controls without settings/install/pack re-sync controls; AI chat personas can use the assistant rail; AI provider managers can access Providers/Defaults without Audit; AI auditors can access Audit without provider/default controls; and `ai.chat` personas without `ai.tools.write` send model requests that include read tools but omit mutating write tools.
Edge Cases: Settings leak to read-only role; install visible without install capability; lifecycle controls shown too broadly; schedule mutation controls shown without lifecycle capability; pack re-sync visible without install capability; AI provider defaults visible to chat-only or auditor role; AI audit visible to provider-manager role; mutating AI write tools exposed to `ai.chat` users without `ai.tools.write`.
Validation Rules: Capabilities are checked by admin access helpers, plugin UI gating, AI route handlers, AI tool-selection gates, and server-side permission checks against granted permissions.
Dependencies: tests/e2e/capabilities.e2e.ts; tests/e2e/ai.e2e.ts; src/admin/pages/plugins; src/admin/ai; server/plugins; server/ai; server/repositories/roles.ts
Known Assumptions: The promoted E2E covers plugin read/install/configure/lifecycle affordance split, installed-plugin schedule read vs lifecycle mutation controls, pack re-sync installer-only affordance, AI chat rail gating, AI provider/audit tab separation, and AI write-tool filtering for a downgraded `ai.chat` persona.
Test Cases: Happy: allowed plugin or AI surface is reachable. Error: forbidden controls hidden. Boundary: read-only plugin browsing and schedule inspection, plugin configure/lifecycle/install/pack isolation, provider-manager/auditor tab isolation, and `ai.chat` without `ai.tools.write`. Invalid: install/settings/lifecycle/schedule mutation/pack re-sync without capability blocked; AI provider/audit tabs absent without matching capability; mutating AI tools absent without write capability. Permission: separate plugin and AI custom personas. Performance: plugin and AI pages render within E2E timeout. Mobile: broader responsive split remains future coverage.
Current Status: Passed 2026-06-23: focused CAP-005 AI write-tool filtering regression and full AI E2E suite passed after prior plugin schedule/pack and full capabilities E2E evidence; CAP-005 listed split coverage is now browser-automated
Notes: Verification: focused `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/ai.e2e.ts -g "chat-only site assistant request omits mutating write tools"` passed 2/2 including setup; full `bun run test:e2e -- --project=e2e tests/e2e/ai.e2e.ts` passed 7/7 including setup. The regression creates a role with temporary provider setup capability, creates a fake Ollama credential as that persona, downgrades the role to `View site` + `Use AI chat`, sends a Site assistant prompt, then parses the provider request to prove `read_document` and `render_snapshot` are present while `insertHtml`, `replaceNodeHtml`, `updateNodeProps`, `applyCss`, `write_code_asset`, `addPage`, and `deletePage` are absent. Prior CAP-005 evidence includes full `tests/e2e/capabilities.e2e.ts` passing 23/23, focused plugin schedule/pack split 5/5, focused AI provider/audit tab split 3/3, and focused plugin action split 5/5. Run logs: docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-capability-responsive-spotlight.md; docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-cap005-ai-provider-audit-capabilities.md; docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-cap005-plugin-action-capabilities.md; docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-cap005-plugin-schedule-pack-capabilities.md; docs/e2e/runs/2026-06-23-cap005-ai-write-tool-filtering.md.

User Story: As an editor using a tablet-width viewport, I want the admin editor chrome, account menu, and canvas to remain usable without overlapping or collapsing.
Expected Behaviour: At 768x1024, the Site editor loads, toolbar is visible, canvas root is visible, and the account menu trigger remains reachable.
Edge Cases: Clipped toolbar; canvas root collapsed; account trigger off-screen; horizontal overflow; panel overlap; session state invalidated by earlier publishing specs.
Validation Rules: Responsive layout is validated through Playwright viewport sizing and visible role/test-id assertions for core editor chrome.
Known Assumptions: The smoke checks the core editor shell at tablet width, not every panel or workflow; phone-width admin authoring remains future exploratory coverage.
Test Cases: Happy: tablet viewport loads Site editor shell. Error: missing toolbar/canvas/account trigger fails. Boundary: 768px viewport. Invalid: unauthenticated access redirects to login outside this row. Permission: owner storage state required. Performance: editor ready within timeout. Mobile: phone-width public page covered by RESP-002.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery
Notes: Verification: combined focused Playwright run passed with no failed tests. Initial combined run exposed an E2E isolation defect where RESP-002 publish step-up rotated the shared owner session before command-palette specs; RESP-002 now uses anonymous storage and fresh login, with no product defect recorded.

User Story: As an editor using Command-K, I want destructive commands to require a second confirmation keystroke so I do not delete content accidentally.
Expected Behaviour: When a destructive command such as deleting the current page is highlighted, the first Enter switches the row into confirmation state and the second Enter performs the action.
Edge Cases: Confirmation not shown; action fires on first Enter; double-fire on second Enter; page tree selection stale after delete; keyboard focus leaves palette.
Validation Rules: Command metadata marks destructive actions; Spotlight state requires an active confirmation before execution and uses keyboard events inside the palette focus trap.
Known Assumptions: The regression uses a disposable page context and one destructive command representative of the destructive-command path.
Test Cases: Happy: first Enter confirms and second Enter deletes. Error: missing confirm fails. Boundary: two sequential Enter presses. Invalid: non-destructive commands should not require confirm. Permission: command availability follows editor capabilities. Performance: palette responds within timeout. Mobile: keyboard desktop path covered; touch path future.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery
Notes: Verification: command-palette.e2e.ts passed inside the combined focused run. SPOT-004 proves the destructive row confirmation copy appears before execution and the command completes only after the second Enter.

User Story: As an editor using Command-K, I want stale destructive confirmations to expire so an old confirmation state cannot execute later by surprise.
Expected Behaviour: After the first Enter starts destructive confirmation, waiting longer than the configured confirmation window clears the confirm prompt and returns the row to normal state.
Edge Cases: Timer fires too early; timer never fires; row stuck in confirm state; second Enter after timeout still executes; focus leaves palette while waiting.
Validation Rules: Spotlight confirmation state has a timeout and is cleared before execution can proceed after expiry.
Known Assumptions: The regression waits beyond the implemented timeout and verifies visible prompt removal; exact millisecond timing is not treated as a product promise.
Test Cases: Happy: confirm prompt disappears after timeout. Error: stale confirm remains. Boundary: wait over 5 seconds. Invalid: second Enter after timeout should require confirmation again. Permission: command availability follows editor capabilities. Performance: timer resolves predictably. Mobile: keyboard path only.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery
Notes: Verification: command-palette.e2e.ts passed inside the combined focused run. SPOT-005 validates destructive confirmation expiry and guards against stale confirmation execution.

User Story: As an admin using Command-K search, I want a clear empty state when my query has no matches so I know the palette is working but there are no results.
Expected Behaviour: Typing a nonsense query with no matching commands shows the empty-state UI with the quoted query and no selectable false-positive result.
Edge Cases: Empty state missing; empty state appears for valid matches; stale prior results remain visible; query text not echoed; keyboard selection points to a non-existent row.
Validation Rules: Spotlight filters command results and renders the empty state only when all providers return no matches for the current query.
Known Assumptions: The browser case uses a deliberately unique query string; async provider delays are covered by SPOT-010.
Test Cases: Happy: nonsense query shows empty state. Error: stale results remain. Boundary: unique long query. Invalid: valid query should not empty. Permission: only commands available to current user are searched. Performance: empty state appears within timeout. Mobile: keyboard path only.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery
Notes: Verification: command-palette.e2e.ts passed inside the combined focused run. SPOT-006 covers the quoted no-results state for a no-match query.

Edge Cases: Selected node not detected; command absent; command ranks too low; stale selection from a deleted node; no-selection context still overboosts layer commands.

Test Cases: Happy: Duplicate layer appears in top results. Error: command missing or unboosted. Boundary: one selected node. Invalid: no selection should not claim layer context. Permission: structure/edit capability controls availability. Performance: ranking computed promptly. Mobile: keyboard path only.

User Story: As an admin who repeatedly uses Command-K, I want recently run commands to appear at the top after reopening the palette so repeat actions are faster.
Expected Behaviour: After running a command, closing and reopening Spotlight with an empty query shows a Recent group containing the prior command near the top.
Edge Cases: Recent command not persisted across opens; duplicate recents; wrong command shown; recent group hidden by stale query; recents leak between users or contexts.
Validation Rules: Spotlight state records successful command execution and renders recents for the active user/session without changing command permission checks.
Known Assumptions: The regression verifies recents within one authenticated browser session; cross-session persistence depth remains lower-level or future coverage.
Test Cases: Happy: run command and see it in Recent on reopen. Error: missing or wrong recent. Boundary: single recent command. Invalid: unavailable commands should not appear as runnable recents. Permission: recents still obey current permissions. Performance: reopen list renders promptly. Mobile: keyboard path only.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery
Notes: Verification: command-palette.e2e.ts passed inside the combined focused run. SPOT-008 covers recent command promotion after execution.

User Story: As an editor working inside the AI assistant panel, I want Command-K to open normally and return focus after closing so panel workflows do not trap keyboard navigation.
Expected Behaviour: With the AI assistant panel open and focused, Command-K opens Spotlight over the panel, keeps panel content behind the backdrop, and Escape closes Spotlight while restoring panel focus.
Edge Cases: Shortcut swallowed by panel; palette opens under panel; focus lost on close; AI panel state resets; Escape closes the wrong layer.
Validation Rules: Global Spotlight shortcut handling coexists with panel focus management and modal layering.
Known Assumptions: The regression uses the Site editor AI assistant panel; other nested panels remain future focus-trap coverage.
Test Cases: Happy: Command-K opens over AI panel and Escape restores focus. Error: shortcut swallowed. Boundary: nested panel focus. Invalid: background panel should not execute commands while palette open. Permission: AI panel visibility follows AI capabilities. Performance: palette opens promptly. Mobile: keyboard desktop path only.

User Story: As an admin searching Command-K, I want asynchronous provider loading to show temporary skeleton rows that resolve into real results so delayed providers feel responsive.
Expected Behaviour: Typing a provider-triggering query shows skeleton rows under the provider group while the request is held, then removes skeletons and shows real results when the provider resolves.
Edge Cases: Skeleton never appears; skeleton never disappears; spinner replaces skeleton design; stale skeletons mixed with results; async error leaves palette stuck.
Validation Rules: Async Spotlight providers expose loading state separately from resolved command rows, and the UI swaps skeleton rows for provider results.
Dependencies: tests/e2e/command-palette.e2e.ts; src/admin/spotlight; server/spotlight or provider route handlers
Known Assumptions: The regression uses the test harness to hold a provider response long enough to observe the skeleton.
Test Cases: Happy: skeleton appears then resolves. Error: stuck skeleton. Boundary: short query triggering provider. Invalid: non-provider query should not show provider skeleton. Permission: provider results still filtered. Performance: loading state appears promptly. Mobile: keyboard path only.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery

User Story: As a keyboard user, I want to open, search, navigate, and run a Spotlight command without the mouse so command workflows are accessible.
Expected Behaviour: From a focused admin control, keyboard shortcut opens Spotlight, typing filters commands, arrow keys move the active row, and Enter executes the selected command without page scroll or form submission behind the palette.
Edge Cases: Arrow keys scroll page; focus escapes input; Enter submits background form; wrong command executes; palette remains open after execution.
Validation Rules: Spotlight traps focus for command navigation and handles keyboard events before background controls receive them.
Known Assumptions: The regression covers one full keyboard command path; broader focus-order sweeps are tracked by A11Y rows.
Test Cases: Happy: open, navigate, and execute command by keyboard. Error: focus escapes or wrong command. Boundary: one arrow navigation. Invalid: Enter with no selected runnable command should not mutate. Permission: only available commands execute. Performance: key handling immediate. Mobile: keyboard path only.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery

User Story: As a user who prefers reduced motion, I want Spotlight open, close, and nested-scope transitions to avoid slide animation so the palette respects my OS accessibility setting.
Expected Behaviour: With reduced motion emulated, opening Spotlight and drilling into a nested scope avoids slide or translateY animation and uses opacity-only or instant transition behavior.
Edge Cases: Reduced-motion preference ignored; slide animation still plays; nested scope animates horizontally; page jumps on open; tests miss animation due timing.
Validation Rules: CSS and state transitions branch on prefers-reduced-motion while preserving palette functionality.
Known Assumptions: The regression uses browser media emulation and style assertions; it does not visually diff every animation frame.
Test Cases: Happy: reduced-motion palette opens and drills into scope without slide animation. Error: transform animation present. Boundary: nested scope transition. Invalid: normal motion mode still allowed separately. Permission: unaffected by capabilities. Performance: no delay introduced. Mobile: reduced-motion media emulation only.

User Story: As a high-contrast user, I want the active Spotlight row and matched text marks to stay visually distinct so search results remain readable.
Expected Behaviour: With high-contrast media emulated, opening Spotlight, searching, and arrowing through results shows a visible active-row outline and readable match marks.
Edge Cases: Highlight invisible; outline too faint; match marks blend into background; keyboard active row differs from visual row; contrast mode breaks selection styling.
Validation Rules: Spotlight CSS uses tokenized high-contrast styles for active rows and marks while preserving keyboard active-state semantics.
Known Assumptions: The regression uses browser media emulation and computed-style checks; full manual visual contrast audit remains future review.
Test Cases: Happy: active row and match marks remain legible. Error: no visible outline or unreadable mark. Boundary: one searched result with arrow navigation. Invalid: high contrast should not hide text. Permission: unaffected by capabilities. Performance: no render delay. Mobile: media emulation only.
Current Status: Focused capability/responsive/spotlight Playwright regression passed 2026-06-23; canonical spreadsheet row added after matrix gap discovery
